name: Vonnie Abercrombie
motto: "To be and not to seem; to do and not to dream."
description: After spending two years at Bessie Tift, Vonnie thought she'd prefer a degree from her State University, and at the end of two years study she has succeeded in obtaining her desire. While here, her friendly way and pleasant smile have won her many friends both among the faculty and students. Although you could never accuse Vonnie of "boning" she has come through with flying colors. Her dislike of flattery, lack of selfishness and love of moderation have always prohibited the "trinket lustre" of worldly show. In the rush and whirl of a busy age such a calm spirit is a rarity. If you just put sympathy, wit, comradeship, originality and fun together you have her. With these qualities in abundance how could we do other than expect great things of her?

name: Horace Isham Abney
motto: "It is not the winning, but how you play, that counts in the game of life."
description: Horace has served his apprenticeship under the auspices of "Little Charlie" and is now prepared to bring fame and glory both to his University and to his native city. As a student he remains without peer. His ability to master the most intricate engineering problems has won for him the regard not only of the faculty but of the student body as well. Horace has reaped honors as well as knowledge and his genial good fellowship and sterling worth has given him a well deserved popularity. Horace has always been a true and trusted friend, and as he goes forth may he find success, a sufficiency of wealth, and favor in his true love's eyes.

name: Walter Stephens Allen, B.S.C.
motto: "Stake your all on the hand that fate deals you."
description: Some men seek the lime-light, some have it turned upon them, but there are still others who are content to work, and to work hard, with no other consolation than that of the grand and glorious work well done. "Zebulon" might well be classed with the latter group. He entered the University in the fall of '21 and, though he has lost one year out of school, he is graduating with the class of '25. During his stay at Georgia Allen has made a record for doing his own work, attending to his own business, and of allowing everyone else to do likewise. After all that is a rare accomplishment and one that might well be envied by some folks. The little village of Zebulon may not be known to all men. Be that as it may; so long as she continues to give to her state such men as the one eulogized herein her immortality is assured.

name: Thomas Lanier Alnutt, B.S.C.
motto: "Every man is the architect of his own fortune."
clubs: First Lieutenant, Infantry, R. O. T. C.
description: "Tommy" came to Georgia in the fall of 1921. He is one of those unassuming chaps, who realizing the need of a conscientious performance in this world to get to the top of the ladder, has been a consistent and dogged worker. Although blessed with a gift of reticence, "Tommy" always has a smile and a kind word for everyone he meets. Aspiring to enter the field of the thickly populated business aggregation, he has worked hard to discipline and align his methods so as to give him an early chance for success. We all feel sure his intense love for business integrity will carry him high up in the world, where only successful men abound.

name: Virginia Easley Athon
motto: "Friendship is the greatest achievement of life."
description: Virginia came to Georgia with a purpose to make good and her two years here can be taken as an indication that she has and will continue to do so. Her hobby is Botany, and her knowledge of that subject is beyond question. She possesses rare charm and attractiveness, and her popularity is not confined to Georgia, for her box is always full of mail from home and ——? She is a person who rarely fails to do what she knows she ought to do, at the time she ought to do it, whether she feels like it or not . . . . except to cut a class.

name: Charles Hubert Baker
clubs: First Lieutenant and Battalion Adjutant, '24 and '25
description: Here is our hero, "Red", as he is familiarly known except to the freshmen and to them it is Professor Baker. "Red" came to us in '21 from the hearty little township of Danielsville, Georgia. Since that time he has mastered most of the figures and notations, better known as "Bull," of the school of commerce. The fact that he was so proficient in these subjects caused him to be appointed instructor in this department during his senior year, and thus he acquired his title of professor. He is also a military genius of rare repute. He is one of the very few who have attended all formations held by the military department during his four years sojourn with us. He is an ardent believer in the slogan, "In time of peace prepare for war." Here's to you, "Red."

name: Fred Harris Bargeron, B.S.A.
motto: "If the elevator to success is not running, take to the stairs."
description: Being the first citizen of Lyons, it is natural to assume that Bargeron would be interested in animal training; on the contrary he has become a poultry expert. His specialty is chicken judging, and he is well qualified for a license. Most of his experience in judging, culling, and selection was acquired at summer schools where his keen eye was always on the alert for a fine specimen. During the hatching season when he is not dashing madly around in his Ford coupe from date to date, he can be found at the State College of Agriculture working on a Candee incubator, which is a contraption calculated to develop modern language not found in the etiquette books. Bargeron, however, does not let difficulties stand in his way, but with energy, grit and ability he forges ahead in anything he undertakes. We feel sure that success will crown any enterprise he undertakes.

name: Mary Elizabeth Barksdale, A.B.
description: William of Stratford dashed off this thistledown of a line, wiped his enchanted goose-quill pen, and smiled his prescient smile as he tossed the phrase across a cool three hundred years and watched it settle lightly upon the head of our Peggy. The youngest? Without a peradventure! And nine? A palpable allusion to the Muses. And a wren? Who, having seen that tiny bird, could fail to call to mind our Peggy—always on the wing, nimble and alert—hovering now and then over some intriguing blossom, to peer into it with bright eyes that sparkle shrewdness, mischief, and intelligence all at once. Brisk and ubiquitous and faithful too. No wonder William smiled—affectionately.

name: Edwin Beer
motto: "A friend may well be reckoned the masterpiece of nature."
clubs: Student Chapter; Am. Sc. C. E.
Captain and Adjutant of Cav. Squadron, '25
description: Edwin distinguished himself by his genial character, his ability to make and keep friends, and best of all, by the pure metal of which he is made. He has studied faithfully for four years and his goal is to put up a structure rivaling New York's best. Go ahead, Edwin, we are for you! We are particularly proud of the executive ability of this young engineer. The only way he does a task, is well and the only way he leaves a task, is finished. And his many friends all admire his moral, upright and gentlemanly character.

name: Leo William Belcher
motto: "Pack up your troubles in the old kit bag, and smile, smile, smile."
description: Leo came from the extreme southwestern section of the state, the land of pork, peanuts and prosperity. He didn't miss being a native of Florida by many miles and some day, if the mad rush to the Everglade State continues, may flit across the line to live among the oranges and the alligators. Leo might have cut a wide path through the field of feminine hearts, but for the fact that he early met his fate and joined the ranks of the matrimonial fraternity. If the advocates of universal peace should fail in their efforts and Uncle Sam should get into a fight, Leo will be found astraddle of a horse in some cavalry division, unless airplanes and poison gas have sent the cavalry to the discard by that time.

name: Flora Blalock
motto: "Great thoughts, like great deeds, need no trumpet." "The eager listener gains what the chatterers lose."
description: Flora received her bachelor's degree at Brenau, where she won laurels in every phase of school life, particularly in Journalism. Having been favorably impressed with the sample of "Georgia" provided at summer school she decided to spend a year with us. Her life at the University has proved a round of pleasures ranging from the festivities of Old English verbs to the frivolities of English Constitutional History, with occasionally something as serious and elevating as "Sally" for a change. Though naturally quiet the twinkle of her black eyes, sometimes only merry, sometimes even wicked, betray her delightful sense of humor. For the benefit of her future pupils, when those same eyes blink three times—Well, you'll see. As a student, intelligent, ambitious, of lofty ideals, she has made a splendid record at the University.

name: John William Blount
motto: "It matters not whether you win or lose, but how you play the game."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Junior Class
description: John—to all his friends "Johnny," and these are numerous—came to the University four years ago; since then he has taken part in all phases of college life. Words cannot describe this magnetic "little" fellow. Anywhere he sees you he greets you with a smile. "Johnny" has been a consistent student and a sincere friend. He is noted for his dogged determination. Never has he despaired of a task no matter how hard it was. Whatever his task might be, he gritted his teeth and with a smile set diligently to work. Determination, affability, conscientiousness, perseverance, and a quick initiative are "Johnny's" main characteristics. We believe he is to make a high mark in this world.

name: Evelyn Baley
motto: "Life is yours—make of it what you will."
description: Evelyn after two years at Brenau decided to get her degree at Georgia. To those who know her casually, she appears lovable, sympathetic, obliging. To those more fortunate who have worked their way deeper into her personality, she shows a disposition deep and broad and fine, and, we must add idealistic. To say that Evelyn will be missed on the campus is needless, for she makes friends very readily and it can truly be said that her words are "neither slow nor few." Possibly this habit has been a partial cause of Evelyn always having to hurry to classes and always getting there late. Being late never seemed to interfere with her work for Evelyn leaves an enviable record behind her.

name: Polly Ruth Bowers
motto: "Not in the clamor of the street, Not in the shouts and plaudits of the throng, But in ourselves, are triumph and defeat."
description: If you've seen a demure little girl with brown eyes and light bobbed hair with the gentlest of manners; if you've seen this girl with her quiet air of determination go about her work and in a calm way accomplish almost anything; if you've seen a girl with a bright, sunny smile that comes in ready response to your lightest moods, or her quick sympathy for your slightest sorrow—then you've seen HER—for there is only one such person on our campus, or in the world for that matter, Polly Ruth Bowers.

name: Louie Render Braswell
motto: "When the ONE great scorer writes against your name, He writes not that you won or lost but how you played the game."
description: Though unable because of a heavy schedule to participate in the many and varied activities that go with college life, Render, through sheer personality and pep, made friends for himself wherever he might be. His personality and indomitable will won a permanent place for him in the confidence and esteem of his fellow students. Though small of stature, he is a man both mentally and physically. While far from being a sheik or tea-hound, yet we have a sneaking fear that he will be missed by more than one of the fairer sex as well as by his classmates when his course is completed. Despite his joy-making and happy spirit he can be serious when the occasion demands. Render hears the call of his profession and is ready to cheerfully answer the roll like the true Georgia man he is, and we feel sure that with his habit of consistent work, and his unfailing determination he will meet with great success.

name: Walter Bridges
clubs: Student Instructor in the Poultry Division
description: A steadier man with a stronger determination to do well, and that which is right, will not be found anywhere in these pages. "Shorty" came to the University with the intention of becoming a chicken expert and this he has succeeded in doing. His scholastic record has been excellent, as shown by the honors which he has attained during his four years here. Gathering information is not all he has succeeded in doing well. He has taken an active part in college activities and has made friends with all whom he has come in contact. To claim Shorty as a friend is to have a friend indeed, and to know him better only makes one appreciate his friendship more. It is useless for us to wish you success Shorty, a man filled with your determination, and desire to play the game fair and square is bound to succeed.

name: Jacob Johnson Butler, B.S.A.
motto: "You are never licked until you quit, don't quit."
description: Jake is one of the best-liked men in college. He is a terrible ladies' man for "he has that merry glance which seldom ladies' hearts resist." He is an all-round jolly good fellow, an ideal college lad, and a perfect gentleman. As an "Athens High School Star" Jake entered the University in the fall of 1921 and he soon discovered that college life consisted of more things than text-books. On the athletic field he has done honor to himself as well as to his Alma Mater. A live wire, charged with energy, friendliness, and native ability, he will never sink into oblivion after his graduation.

name: James Villard Calhoun, B.S.C.
motto: "A man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's his heaven for?"
description: Get ready to speak folks—here comes Jimmie Calhoun. Yes sir, in the last four years every time Henry Ford has made a flivver James has made ten friends. In his sophomore year he ran second in the ugly man's contest and was leading until the last ten minutes. Quite an honor for this young man because leaving all joking aside he is really a good looking, well dressed young man. Many students are called the Co-ed's delight but Jimmie is sweet papa to them all. He has a whiskey tenor and sings on all occasions. Coming from the Boys' High School of Atlanta in '21 Jimmie has made a great record. Although weighing around 220 pounds he is very graceful, girls—has cat eyes, a little black hair, several teeth, and is pretty good looking, that is, he doesn't scare children. He knows all the Costa family and can tell them apart. If a success in life can be made, Jimmie Calhoun will certainly make one.

name: ALBERT JULIUS DORNBLATT, B.S.
motto: "Does else than noble service count; To us, else than noble deeds to anything amount?"
description: The boy with the "million-dollar smile," that's Al. Walking a mile for a Camel is nothing to what a member of the fair sex would walk only to behold his smile. But then too, he's a "man's man" in its every meaning. When he is present anywhere you can feel assured that joy will reign supreme and that some very interesting question is in the process of discussion. His three years' collegiate record is worthy of emulation. Honors have come to him, though he never deliberately sought them. They came as a natural result of worthy efforts.

name: LEON SELLERS CARTER, B.S.A.
description: Carter hails from the Palmetto state and from the city of BEAUFORT. During the war he gladly came to the aid of his country and in action he incurred injuries that will be a constant reminder of those horrifying days. He has the esteem of all those whom he comes in contact with, and for one to have him as a friend is indeed fortunate. During his sojourn here he has accomplished what it takes the average fellow four years to do, but he has successfully qualified for his degree in three years. To see him about his work one would take him to be single, but at home we find a family of four that gather around the fire at night and share life together. Carry your high aims and ambitions onward to success as you have laid the foundation diligently. The good wishes from your many friends shall always be with you.

name: Robert S. Caruthers
motto: "It is better to have tried and failed, than never to have tried at all."
description: Rabbit, as he is familiarly known, is a product of Statesboro, and it should be proud to call him so. As a real man with the girls he is hard to beat. Treat 'em rough and tell 'em nothing, is, we believe the reason for his success. Rabbit has made a large number of friends while in college, and at the end of four years of association many of us can say that he is a man truly worth knowing. Rabbit is a rather good student, a good sport, and "an all around good fellow." He has the qualifications required for a successful life, and with a personality as pleasing as his, and the natural gift of making friends, we are all expecting great things from him in life.

name: Ardy Homer Chambers
motto: "To see ourselves as others see us; to be ourselves as others would be us."
clubs: Fresh. and Soph. Ag. Club Debate
description: "Andy," so-called by his legion of friends, has achieved many honors and distinctions since entering our University. He is every inch a man; a worthy leader, and a tried and true friend. Not only is "Andy" an exceptionally fearless, conscientious, determined worker, but he is a gentleman in the best accepted sense of that term. He has won his degree in three years which shows that he possesses intellectual ability and capacity much above the average. A man with his record has a great future ahead. So here's to "Andy," who finds no task unworthy and no responsibility too great.

name: Isaac Vivian Chandler
description: "Fat," as he is commonly called, has taken part in most every college activity, and even though he has never been a caller at the co-ed barn he has used the library to the best advantage. A glance above will convince one that "Fat" has won many honors during his sojourn here, but his greatest attainments have been in the field of debating, having won the distinction of placing on every debate tried for.

name: Mattie Mae Chapman
motto: "It's the songs ye sing and the smiles ye wear, that's a-makin' the sun shine everywhere."
enrolltime: She entered Georgia as a Junior
description: Mattie Mae came to us from the State Normal School. She entered Georgia as a Junior, to continue her preparation for the teaching profession. She is sure to succeed, for with her spirit of interest and determination she becomes a part of her work. Mattie Mae enjoys her college life. She is a girl we all love for her true worth, and is an unfailing friend to those who are so fortunate as to gain her friendship. She always has a group of girls around her, for her happy spirit and wholesome fun is enjoyed by all. Our wish for Mattie Mae is that she will continue to be a blessing to those she comes in contact with as she has been to us here at Georgia.

name: George Miller Clarke
motto: "Give to the world the best you have and the best will come back to you."
clubs: Sergeant in R. O. T. C. Infantry
First Lieutenant R. O. T. C. Infantry
First Lieutenant R. O. T. C. Infantry, Company B
description: Flute came to Georgia from Richmond Academy, long famed for capable graduates; and he has not disappointed his Alma Mater. During his college years, Miller has been a leader in Y. M. C. A. and other Christian work on the campus, a member of the Y. M. C. A. Cabinet and of the Student Fellowship group. Capable, steadfast, faithful and conscientious, yet fun-loving and popular with his fellow students, you have our best wishes for your success in life, Miller. As you leave college to conquer the world of engineering, carry with you the satisfaction which comes from having done your work well; and remember that "life is yours—make of it what you will."

name: David Lowe Cloud
clubs: First Lieutenant R. O. T. C.
description: This illustrious young gentleman, whose beauty is of the Latin type, is one of the most versatile members of this year's graduating class. He has won fame as a student, as an athlete, as a military genius and as a world's traveler. And lastly it might be said of him that his being is inculcated with those innate qualities which will ever and anon hold him before the world as a gentleman. "Doc" entered Georgia in the fall of 1921 and his congenial personality and sense of fair play in all his endeavors early endeared him to his classmates, and his compatriots have found him to be a most adept dispeller of blues when their days were darkest and their trials and cares heaviest. Our hero is undecided at present as to just what line of business activity he will tackle after commencement but his friends feel sure that he will meet with brilliant and scintillating success. And their best wishes will follow him.

name: Harriet Elizabeth Colbert, A.B., Ed.
motto: "Don't let your education interfere with your schooling."
description: No one would have thought that little cherubic Harriet would have become one of our dignified Seniors, but such she is. Our first impression of Harriet was that she was quiet and shy, but she is becoming one of our most eloquent talkers and has been known to talk three hours on Botany without a rest of more than fifteen minutes. In the two years she has been at Georgia we have found her easy-going, jolly, good-natured and always wearing a smile. And since friendliness is next to goodliness, Harriet proves an agreeable companion any time, anywhere and is liked and admired by all. "Pleasure fills our youthful years, Why study if it interferes?"

name: Lottie Lorine Collins, B.S.H.E.
description: After six years in Coweta County as Home Demonstration Agent during which time she was busy opening the door of opportunity for the club girls under her guidance, Lorine herself stepped over the threshold of old Georgia. Wherever a group of students is gathered having fun Lorine is found in the midst, and yet one always realizes she is laughing with her, not at her. Her most loved occupation is reading Woodstock and Kenilworth; her most arduous task that of serving as proctor in Soule Hall. Her goal for term grades is best understood by her motto. Although never too busy to play a prank Lorine enters into her work with the right spirit, and without boasting accomplishes what she sets out to do. With a generous nature, a sympathetic spirit, high ideals, and the courage of her convictions Lorine will go out to find fields of ever greater service. "None knew her but to love her, None named her but to praise."

name: Carlton Black Colquitt
motto: "I fear nothing but poverty and ignorance."
description: In his very beginning Carlton was enabled to place a grip of steel upon the years that were to come, for the ability to see a distant goal and bend all things toward it was his by right of an iron will, the only true mark of genius. Thrown upon his own resources he laid a firm hand upon the future and proceeded to formulate his own destiny. The success so far of his endeavor is measured in part by four splendid years at college and final graduation. As items of little importance, besides this stands a list of singular honors that accumulated over his head, while he went about the main task of cultivating to the highest standards, an impressive mind, a broad understanding, and a nature with the finest feelings.

name: Myer Sussman Cook
motto: "I will find a way or make one."
description: Mike comes to us from Fitzgerald, a place affectionately known as "the magic city." Possessed of an amiable disposition, Mike has made many friends during his sojourn on the Georgia campus. He has been a diligent worker and a consistent performer and we are sure that if he follows that policy through life, he shall be successful. Mike leaves here after three years thoroughly imbued with the Georgia spirit. He came here with business on his mind and he goes into the world as he came here. He has, of course, at times, felt the pierce of cupid's arrows. They all do. Mike is a person of much capacity and ability. During his eventful career at college this talent and ability of Mike's has been demonstrated in many ways. We predict that in the future he will succeed and be a credit to his Alma Mater.

name: Simon Nabhen Courie
motto: "To succeed one must do a little more than is required of him."
description: Truly Simon is one in a thousand. He came to the University under the disadvantage of being a foreign-born lad, but somehow it seems that he has made disadvantages into advantages. For it is truly a distinction to be known as thoroughly over the University as has been Simon. But it would not be so remarkable if it stopped there, but scarcely is there a girl at the Normal School, or a citizen in Athens who could not call him by name. He doesn't talk of one girl long enough at a time to let any secrets out, but you never can tell. Anyway they lead the best of us astray at unguarded moments. And especially Simon, do they like, those who play a good hand of bridge. Seriously we don't know of a man in the class whom we would rather meet in after years for a genuine good old "bull session."

name: Patrick Lee O'Neal Crenshaw
motto: "Determination and action are the two main factors of success."
description: Patrick Lee O'Neal Crenshaw, better known as "Pat", is from the Gate City of the South. After spending two years at Oglethorpe University, he entered the University of Georgia in the fall of 1923. He possesses more outstanding characteristics than any student in the university. He has specialized in various and sundry subjects such as Commerce and Socialism. "Pat" spent his high school days at Boys High School of Atlanta, graduating in 1921. During his college career he has been faithful to his studies. Nature has been very grateful to this man, bestowing upon him the art of argumentation by which he has downed many great men in the past. He has a great determination to get what he wants with results. "Pat" expects to study law at the University of Virginia. May success and blessings greet him while on this old terrestrial ball.

name: James Alex Cromartie
motto: "Trust no one and you will not be deceived."
description: "Jimmie" comes from a South Georgia town known as Vidalia. We don't know so much about the place, but judging from the representative it sends here we think it must be "quite the berries." Jimmie has not put out so much energy trying to win every honor in school, but has proven that this is not the only way to become popular on the campus. From the beginning of his first year he entered into the spirit of college life with full force, joining every "bull session" enthusiastically but in such a way as to increase the number of his friends. It seems that he has been able to fit every phase of college life, and we frankly believe that he will do as well when he enters into his life profession, and if he does there is no chance but that he will become one of the most popular in the business world. Let us give him credit for what he has done here, and imagine for ourselves what he will do hereafter.

name: Charles Franklin Crouch
motto: "Be not surprised at what your friends may do."
description: In the fall of '21, Frank left the little city of Ocilla and entered the University to equip himself for his voyage over the rugged sea of life. This he has done in a manner that makes us all admire him. Having spent four years in Candler Hall he has enjoyed the full benefit of the old Georgia Spirit which we love so well. Frank's high ambition has not been in making high marks, memorizing theories, and deriving formulae, but to know and practice the use of common sense. This is one of the reasons why he has so many friends, why he was elected president of the Freshman class, and why he would have been elected to numerous other positions had he so desired. He is a man of high morals, of strong character, of consistent efforts, and a man whom his Alma Mater will always be proud of. The best wishes of his many friends go with him into the business world.

name: Charles Arthur Curtis
motto: "Always take more than you can do, and then do it."
description: Ladies, just look at the beaming countenance depicted above and you will see the champion of women's rights at the University of Georgia. Behold a man who believes that women have rights and even a certain amount of native intelligence as well. "Curt" hails from the hills far back in Pike county. They call the place Neal. "Curt" came to Georgia with grim determination in evidence over his entire anatomy. This same spirit has marked all of his endeavors at college as is evidenced by the fact that he has proved himself a leader in everything that he has undertaken. He leads his class in Civil Engineering and bids fair to become one of our foremost structural geniuses in the future.

name: Joel Dean
motto: "Do not stare up the steps of success but step up the stairs."
enrolltime: During his three years' stay in our midst
description: Oh, readers, gaze upon the above and you will see the fair, curly locks that the fair ones "dote" upon. When he passes by on the campus with his hat off, all the co-eds within seeing distance heave a sigh of admiration which is bordering on adoration. But, Curly has inside his cranium something else that attracts besides visible beauty. He has brains—as is demonstrated by the scholastic honors that are his. He possesses an enviable trait—the ability of keeping out of other people's business save when requested. During his three years' stay in our midst, he has not only won the esteem and respect of all with whom he has come in contact, but made for himself a host of friends. Luck to you, Curly! May the cup of plenty overflow for your benefit.

name: Winifred Jordan Davis
description: "Winnie" is one of those persons who win you to them in spite of the methods you may employ to resist. With such personality and charm I guess she isn't responsible for that, for she has in addition those admirable qualities of self-assurance, dignity, independence, ability, and wit. Winifred has made her own place in the "Hall of Fame" and doesn't need me or anyone to make it for her. But it is certain that never from her lips will you ever hear about it. When it comes to one's self, she is the most conservative person that ever walked the campus. If, as they say, "the world's greatest treasures are friends," it is certain that Winifred will never be in want. Our very best wishes go with her when she leaves us, and even now we're wondering what we are gonna do without her! I am afraid it "can't be did."

name: John Milton Day
motto: "You are never defeated until you quit."
description: During his four years here "Milt" has devoted his interest to a variety of achievements. Studies, college activities, and social activities. In the first he is one of the leaders in his class, in the second he is a wearer of the "G", and the winner of the cup presented to the best horseman in the cavalry unit, and a possessor of numerous other awards of merit. In the third, we hear he is a friend of the ladies. And with the male students we know of no man who has more real friends. One of the things to be admired about "Milt" is the fact that he has never allowed petty politics to soil his palm, but has directed his consistent efforts toward things that are really worth while. Another thing we admire of him is his great love and appreciation of the Georgia spirit. And in the words of the Athens Banner-Herald, after his memorable speech at the football banquet, "May the spirit of Milton Day live on and on to inspire the men and women of old Georgia."

name: Ernest G. Dickey
motto: "A poor beginning is better than none at all."
description: "Count" Dickey is one of those personages so rarely found in these days of the eternal search after gold—a gentleman of the old plantation school. A man who has amassed no great fund of those hollow things called honors but a man who has an even greater claim to being a big man in that he holds a big place in the heart of all who know him. The "Count" has that peculiar faculty of apparently taking things easy and enjoying life while in reality he is getting a lot of good work done. Though from one of our big centers of population in the East he became a Georgia man, in every sense of the word, over night. We respect him as much as we love him and know that if he continues to live as he has lived that life will be as sweet for him as it has been for those who have known him.

name: Ernest C. Dietz
motto: "I desire to be a friend to all worthy men, and dwell in peace with humanity."
description: "Daddy," as he is called by his many friends came to us in the fall of 1922. He, as his name implies is the proud father of three little children. After spending sixteen months in the service of Uncle Sam "Daddy" decided to go to college and we are proud that he selected Georgia as his Alma Mater. We feel that he is a man of whom we will be proud to own as ours when he is gone. He is never seen without his sunny smile and word of good cheer. He has applied himself well and we predict for him a success in whatever line of work he may choose. What a Paradise this world would be if all men were as jolly and as good natured as "Daddy." So here is to you "Daddy" and may our best wishes go with you.

name: Ada Bernice Echols
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Student Government of Women, 1924-25
description: Bernice first came to Georgia in January of 1913 to the first Club Girls' Short Course. From that time the "Spirit of Old Georgia" has called her. Through grammar school, high school, State Normal and several years of teaching the call came clearer and clearer until in the fall of 1923 she answered with a ring of enthusiasm and loyalty. These two years have been full of joy in the service of her Alma Mater and her classmates and a host of friends are watching her with warm sympathy and keen interest as she goes back into the field with a broader vision and a greater opportunity for service to the state.

name: Homer Christine Eberhardt
motto: "It may be so, but it ain't all so."
description: When the sands of the desert grow cold And the dictum of Barnum hangs fire, There shall be no moaning at the bar When Barney comes into his own. Down to the sea in ships, Far from the hills of Habersham I shall rush out as I am without one plea To prosecute the innocents abroad, To defend the beautiful and damned. Fade me! Fade me! Shower down boy. For it is written, that one shall come Defending Loeb and Leopold. Yes, show me the rocks,—bullion,—scad,—kale,— Banknotes,—dough,—and filthy lucre. Absotively, Mr. Gallagher? Postolutely, Mr. Shean!

name: Sara Elder
motto: "Call me your friend is all I would ask."
description: When we think of Sara we have visions come to our minds of all that is beautiful and wonderful. She has been taught the aesthetic values of a sweet disposition and radiates sunshine wherever she goes. She can be a leader and still keep her friends. But now her college days are over and we must lose her. We will miss her but memories of the days when she was with us will recall those sweet things which were continually happening to make us love her more and more. When she goes out from our flock she will attract friends to her—more and more—for her power to attract friends has grown since we have known her. Sara has done well at the University—scholastically and socially. Her time spent here can be called highly successful and nothing but the brightest sort of a future can be predicted for her.

name: Rutherford Lipscomb Ellis
motto: "Even a postage stamp knows enough to stick it out."
clubs: Executive Committee of the University Y. M. C. A.
description: About four years ago there came among us a bald-headed freshman, timid and unheralded. During this short span of four years Ruddy has accomplished much, in Athletics, in social activities, and as representative of a real man. The Student Council has selected him as its secretary and treasurer, he represents his fraternity on the Pan-Hellenic Council, and is a member of the Executive Committee of the University Y. M. C. A., being its secretary. Good bye, old boy, you have been a real man and a true friend.

name: Troy England Evitt, A.B.
motto: "Mind your own business and expect others to do likewise."
enrolltime: entered the University four years ago
description: "Red," who is from the rolling hills of north Georgia, entered the University four years ago. He was graduated from the Berry School, of Rome, where he early learned that the world could not be conquered without education. Troy did not come to college, however, with the measly idea that books played the entire game in education, but has taken part in all college activities. He has done well in his scholastic work, but besides he has made a number of honors worthy of mention. "Red" has been prominent in his literary society, made several honorary fraternities, and is one of the best track men in this "ole" southland; having the highest distinction his team could award by electing him captain. We must also mention that "Red" is a real shiek with the ladies, and it is said that he never misses an opportunity to hear the S. N. S. Glee Club.

name: Charles Clare Fargo, B.S.C.
description: Charlie is one of these boys who do not have very much to say because they have so much to do that they want done well. He is a student of Commerce and is interested in every phase of it. He does not bewail the faults of the world, neither does he praise it. But all in all he is an optimist and is on the right road. Charlie is also very careful in his work. He takes into consideration all that has happened in the past and uses it for a basis for what may happen in the future. He profits by the mistakes he may have made and sees that it does not happen again. He will succeed whether fortune comes his way or not. He is ready for a patient struggle and reward will come to him in the long run.

name: Loyd Farmer
motto: "It isn't doing what we like to do, but liking what we have to do that makes work a pleasure."
description: What brought Loyd to the University away from the scenic mountaineer section of north Georgia has ever been a complex to us. But what has been far more astounding is how he has stayed here so long. Though each September brought his pleasant presence back among us much as the proverbial kitten. To know him is to like him and to like him is to like him more. His features are hard and so is his head but "One cannot judge grapefruit by its peeling." Therefore let not his countenance deceive you, for beneath those rugged features there lies the heart of a man. Loyd, old egg, it's useless for us to wish for you success, for success to a man like you is forthcoming, and we only hope that you will find therein the satisfaction that you seek.

name: Mary Olivia Ferguson
motto: "Happiness grows at our own firesides, and is not to be picked in strangers' gardens."
description: The gods were kind to her. Her alma mater and her fellow-students having fittingly bestowed upon her many well-deserved honors. "Mary Ferg" has found her greatest joy in willing service. A marvelous mixture of the ideal and the practical; of common sense and imaginative fancy; she has gained much in her four years of loyal devotion to her high ideals of scholarship and she has lost not a bit of the dignity and simplicity, the poise and charm of manner with which she came among us as a naive freshman.

name: Luke Austelle Forrest
motto: "They who know truth are inferior to those who love truth."
description: Some men invite glory with open arms. Others retire modestly, seeing in the limelight something that smacks of vulgar ostentation. To the latter class belongs Forrest. He enlisted, served with distinction during the World War, was seriously wounded; but he has never advertised the facts. A good student, organizer of a chemistry seminar, writer of scientific articles, in his collegiate and social activities he maintains a like modesty. "The path of glory lead but to the grave." Forrest is specializing in plant chemistry. He will do additional work at the University of Georgia next year and afterwards plans to spend two years at Johns Hopkins, followed by special courses at the University of Berlin. His aim is later to combine teaching and research in chemistry.

name: Byard Owens Fry
motto: "It is not what you think of yourself, but what others think, that counts."
enrolltime: entered the University five years ago; joined our class when we were Juniors
description: "Friday" entered the University five years ago to continue his studies along agricultural lines which he started in high school. After two years he had to drop out of College, but after teaching a year, "Friday" being determined to finish his college course, joined our class when we were Juniors, and it was indeed a pleasure for us to welcome such a man to our class because he is the kind to practice, not preach, his convictions. "Friday" is one of the few students that when given work has always given it the best that he had and never waited for some one else to do it. We feel sure that he is the kind of man that the world needs more of today. Here's to you, "Friday," and if you hit 'em in the future as you have since you have been with us, we feel sure success will be your reward.

name: Elbert William Gallaher
motto: "To help others is to help yourself, and the pleasure that comes to those who help constitutes one of the true bases of human happiness."
description: Although he receives his checks from West Palm Beach, Florida, now, Eb's real home is in Montezuma, Georgia, for it is from there that he first came to the University. This young man has gone through his university life with the same determination and consistency that characterize his efforts in any line of endeavor; the result, an admirable college career. Eb has made good marks always, though not especially high ones; he is an orator of parts, a tennis player of note, being finalist in the elimination tourney; popular socially, and a great fellow personally. Engaged in various types of college activities, he has allowed none of his interests to completely dominate him and so has developed all-around.

name: Gay Taylor Gard
description: Stop, look. Cast your eyes—gaze, behold, view, scrutinize, examine, and you will see one of the products of the mountains. "G. G." is truly a student of the old school, completing in three years a four-year course in horticulture, and is now out in his "Ford" looking for new worlds to conquer. His smiles and warm hand-shakes have won for him a circle of true friends and a host of acquaintances. He is an ideal lover for all the summer-school girls and a shiek with the Co-Eds. His ideals are high and "G. G." has the manhood to attain them. "Old Boy," we wish you happiness, full-rounded happiness, and the joy of achievement that comes with success.

name: Leslie Sears Gardiner
motto: "To do your duty on all occasions calls forth the best that is in a man."
enrolltime: After four years at Richmond Academy he comes to Georgia
description: This is the little fellow that few of the men and none of the ladies have heard of until now. He is no less than "Sears." He hails from Augusta, "the town of he-men and she-women." After four years at Richmond Academy he comes to Georgia in search of new worlds to conquer and duties to perform. He came, he saw and now he has conquered. "Sears" is a man beyond reproach, even beyond the ladies. This little fellow is small but he has hit all the professors hard. All he asks for is his pipe and a job to do and he will do it. He has won fame as proctor of Milledge Hall. Every one there claims him as a friend; to count him your friend is a compliment. We are expecting great things from you, "Sears," and will always treasure your friendship at Georgia.

name: Mahlon Cooper Garrett
motto: "He most lives who thinks most, feels the noblest, acts the best."
enrolltime: Garrett has been a resident of the Campus for four years
description: This young man from Walton County has spent the greater part of his life, thus far, on the farm. There he learned that it requires work to accomplish things, and with that idea in mind he has made his college course a business. However, like most boys on the farm, he still believes in "knocking off" for Saturday afternoon when he takes a bath (not in the old swimming hole at horse-shoe bend), puts on his Sunday "store-bought" clothes and goes to see his best girl or to enjoy the old "Virginia reel." Garrett has been a resident of the Campus for four years, where he has established a broad acquaintance and where he is admired and respected by all who know him. He especially deserves a great deal of credit because he has pursued his course on his own financial resources; with his ambition and determination he is destined to become a valuable man in the commercial world.

name: Owen Edward Gay, B.S.A.
motto: "It's the good you do in the world that counts, not what you get credit for doing."
description: Just an ideal Georgia student, I would call him. Besides making an excellent record in the class-room he has taken an active part in various lines of college activities at the same time, which fits him out a well-rounded man. Gay is known by every Georgia student, and it can be truthfully said that no one in college has made more friends than the one you see pictured above. The name "Gay" fits him exactly for he is a true optimist in every sense of the word. Always a cheerful look and a friendly word for all with whom he meets, causes him to be liked by every one. Georgia is proud of you, Gay. Your record here has been excellent, and we are sure that you will continue to be successful in later life. Go forth into the world and make the success you have attained here and you will make Georgia a useful and worthy citizen.

name: Bishop Franklin Grant, B.S.F.
motto: "Preach very little but practice that which is preached."
description: Who is this quiet, brown-eyed, almost bald-headed chap who enjoys such intimate fellowship with his corn-cob pipe? Who is this fellow who says so little; who never speaks unless he knows what his speech concerns; whose very soul seems to be revealed, and in that revelation discloses nothing but honesty, fidelity, constancy and sincerity, with never a trace of menial qualities? Who is this congenial, attentive student, who so unobtrusively engenders confidence and affection? Who is this unruffled philosopher who reasons out tremendous truths, and, feeling the futility of expounding them, is content to be burdened with them? Bishop Grant is unknown to many people. He has seen fit to walk quietly amid the confusion of college life and to practice that which is practical instead of squabbling over the impractical theories. Never has his voice clamored amid the monstrous howling.

name: Granath, Iver Nimrod Von Henry
motto: "The truth is that falsehood which makes the greatest impression on us."
enrolltime: Two years ago I left Tech
clubs: Alpha Wun and Wunfa Al (Honors?) Gridiron Club
description: Now shout your curses, "Suicist, Individualist, Weist, Solipsist, Egotist," for I am the master of my own writeup. Two years ago I left Tech because I couldn't take it with me. I came to the University and told everybody that I was a conceited ass and they took my word for it, but should I bow my head over such a trival thing? Not on your suspenders—I get a tremendous kick out of "Suicist, Individualist," et cetera. You call me erratic; praise to Allah, I am. Ethics is my law—sincere pursuit of aesthetic truth my ambition. Now laugh that off.

name: Green, Calvin Davis
motto: "Make up your mind definitely to do a thing and the battle is half won."
description: It can be said of Calvin that he possesses those desirable qualities of that great man who has the same name. Unlike Coolidge, however, he does not aspire to the "White House." C. D's. dreams center about the "Little Gray Home in the West," with a "Sweet Adeline" to whom he may sing "I Love You," for Green, you know, is an accomplished lov—er—er, we mean musician. And he has lent his vocal talent unstintingly during the time of his college career to religious, educational and civic organizations. Green has had the double duty of both laying a foundation for higher education and of building thereon. Nevertheless, he has been a worthy steward of the opportunities offered by an appreciative Uncle Sam to those men whose brave and sacrificial service overseas merited even more. Green has never been too busy though, to be a friend—a smile here, a cheery greeting there, and often a lift (in his tin lizzie).

name: Lewis Crouch Green, B.S.A.
motto: "To do what ought to be done, but what would not be done unless I did it, I thought to be my duty."
description: Being somewhat quiet and conservative by nature, "Reb," as he is known to his more intimate friends, is that kind of boy who does not make friends with everybody at first sight; but the more you associate and become acquainted with him the more you like him and come to appreciate those sterling qualities that go to make up a true friend. The esteem by which he is held in the mind of his fellow-students is plainly demonstrated by the fact that he has been chosen as president of one organization in the University, namely the Horticultural Club, and is a member of the Cavalier Social Club. Besides these two high honors, he won his numerals in Freshman basketball and at present is a member of the Student Council.

name: Thomas Fitzgerald Green, IV, A.B.
motto: "No pleasure under the sun Equals that of work well done."
clubs: Winner of Sophomore Cup for Declamation
Vice-President University of Georgia Play Folk
President of Phi Kappa Literary Society
description: Four years ago there registered on the books of this University a small boy who was destined to be one of the greatest literary speakers of our class. President of Phi Kappa, along with many other speaking places is certainly a great record. Besides making all the honorary societies he turns around and takes law on the side while he graduates with an A.B. degree.

name: Annie McGaha Griffin
description: Anne Griffin is a synonym for enigma. Her thoughts are not discernible, nor her actions predictable, and therein lie her individuality and her charm. Of a philosophic temperament, she thinks, but talks little, not presuming that mankind would be benefited by opinions that she might flaunt. In addition to rare intellectual ability, Anne has an enthusiasm, a light-hearted gaity, and a lovable feminine nature that make her one of unusual charm.

name: James Harold Hancock
motto: "Dream and not make dreams your master."
clubs: Junior Chapter American Society of Civil Engineers
description: The curly-headed boy above is the man Diogenes was looking for, and, looked too soon. Athens boasts of this unique character, and, besides being the fairest and most sincere chap you ever met, he is a good student and a musician. He is an ardent sportsman in every sense of the word and believes in always meeting you over half way. He has a way with the ladies all his own. "Music hath charms to sooth the savage beast," you know. We are losing a man we cannot replace, but we must give our best in helping the progress of the world. So we wish him as much success in his future undertakings as he has had in the past.

name: Ethel Elizabeth Hailey
motto: "Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice."
description: Elizabeth, on graduating from Athens High School showed her "Georgia spirit" when she turned down a scholarship to Lucy Cobb to enter the University as a freshman. Some may think Elizabeth rather reserved but those who really know her are ever-conscious of her charm and strength of character. In her demure and unassuming manner she has won her way into the hearts of many. "Miss Hailey" held a responsible position as student-assistant in the Library throughout her senior year. "A nature generous, a spirit fine A happy heart, a soul divine." This describes our Libby, and as "sincerity is the basis of all true success," she will succeed in whatever she undertakes.

name: Lester Hargrett
motto: "Good may come, but I doubt it."
description: To sketch a person unmistakably in two hundred words is generally something close to impossible. Most characters and most faces are appallingly similar—they lack distinction. Only with difficulty can the Delineator catch the unique features which make a man himself and nobody else. To strike off a likeness of Lester Hargrett is relatively easy. Watch how the mere thinking on this man guides inevitably the lines of my pencil.

name: James Blackshear Harley
motto: "To believe in the life of love, honor and truth."
description: Jim has distinguished himself as a student, as a man, as a musician, and as an athletic manager. He is rounding out four years attendance at Georgia and it can truthfully be stated that they have been very beneficial years to Jim and also to his fellow-students. He has always been a source of pleasure and benefit to them. His ever-present and ever genial smile, and his kind words of salutation have ever and anon been a gloom dispeller when things about them were darkest and they had lost hope in the adage, "There is a silver lining in every cloud" or words to that effect.

name: Katie Harris
motto: "Away with care—Let worry hie him hence."
description: A pair of twinkling brown eyes, a sunny smile, and a happy-go-lucky air—well, that's Katie. At a glance one would hardly think that this jolly, vivacious maid was equally proficient in the culinary arts and in leading the Homecon Club through its most successful year. During her sojourn at Georgia, Katie has been the best kind of a student, participating in all the activities and still having plenty of time for moon-gazing on the fire escape and feasts after lights, though often times her characteristic giggle has been enough to betray her as well as the whole bunch. But as luck would have it, Katie always managed to come out on top. A fun-loving disposition and versatile ability, combined with determination, will carry Katie to her goal in life.

name: William Pratt Heath, Jr.
motto: "The only way to have a friend is to be one."
description: A wealth of rare good sense, the charm of engaging social graces, a happy blending of Cavalier and Puritan, all mingled with the milk of human kindness—that's Bill Heath. Bill has lived among these academic halls for three years, pursuing the coveted title of Bachelor of Arts. These years have brought to light a richly varied personality; for his was a disposition "leaning toward the sunny side," and his was a mind both keen and receptive, alert and equal to the responsibilities of life. Successful we shall call him—for what other label befits a man who has achieved his aim, has won and held the priceless gold of friendship, and acquired that paramount end of all education, a discerning and disciplined mind? As he embarks upon a career that promises rich usefulness, a devoted host of comrades will wave him a hearty "bon voyage!"

name: Edna Hendricks
motto: "The aim, if reached or not, makes great the life."
clubs: Senior Representative of Girls' House Council
description: Edna came to us after running (around) G. S. C. W. two years. At Georgia she pursued the same tactics, and her winning personality has endeared her to the hearts of a host of friends both among the faculty and students. We just can't understand how Edna can take half the courses on the campus, all in the same year, make no less than 90 in them, and still have time for so much devilment. Quick mind? Yes, indeed, one realizes that after five minutes' conversation. You should hear her jokes. Makes no difference if she does always forget the point, they're funny, anyway. Phi Kappa Phi noticed her brilliant record and invited her to membership this year. We hope for her many other honors as she goes through life.

name: John Harrison Hosch, B.S.C.
motto: "You just can't tell a thing about it."
description: Three years ago he was only another freshman, one among many, unknown and insignificant. Today he is honored and esteemed as one of "Georgia's" worthiest sons. "Johnnie" is not only a scholar and a soldier of superior ability, but he has also won distinction as an orator and debater. Looking back upon such a past, on which nothing but the picture of success and achievement is painted in glorious colors, one cannot but be led to expect still greater accomplishments when this illustrious personage enters into the more serious tasks of life.

name: William Robert Humphreys, A.B., Ed.
description: "Bobby", possibly better known as "The Little Colonel", hails from Moultrie, and comes to us from North Georgia "Aggie" College, where true soldiers of fame and fortune are reared. His chivalrous traits, pleasing personality, and keen sense of duty make him worthy of the title he bears. Self-styled, as a woman-hater, "Bobby" has perplexed his friends by numerous nocturnal visitations to the "Co-Ed-Barn". We hope that the outcome of his career at "Georgia" will be as successful as the remembrance of him will ever be pleasant to his friends.

name: Ben Howard Husbands
motto: "To exalt the fundamental virtues of true friendship."
description: "Ben" surely deserves the compliment of truth, "He is every inch a man". He has a most highly developed personality, possessing the virtues of a true gentleman—a natural ability for making friends and holding them. No gift could be more precious. His career at Georgia is closed with a high social prestige. We can't say that his scholastic duties have been pursued with—alacrity, but always he reaches his goal, and along with that a splendid record in outside activities. These attributes combined make him the well known and admired "Ben" that we know.

name: Nannie Ella Ivey
description: Nan is the youngest and tenderest "shoot" of the Boston Iv(e)y, she having reached out a tendril from the family tree in Boston (Georgia) several years ago. Her ingenuity and adaptability showed themselves at a very early age when she was caught using someone's choice tooth brush in her pedicure. Her growth would not have been stunted had she not missed certain exercises, due to her fond parents and the supply of peachtrees and brushes being exhausted by her "twin" sister (several years her senior). Although clever, capable and conscientious, she attempts to camouflage the fact by appearing to take life very lightly. Nan has the rare gift of making friends with no apparent effort, and she has the happy faculty of making the most of a situation. Were she marooned on a desert island, she would doubtless ere long have the monkeys ministering to her comfort and amusement.

name: Greene Flournoy Johnson, B.S.C.
clubs: Social Editor of the Red and Black, '22-'23
description: To Monticello goes the honor of sending Greene to the University. Before coming to the University he received his prep school training at the Stanton Military Academy. After a sojourn there of several years, Greene entered the University in the fall of 1921. His general average is among the highest in the Commerce Department. This year his average is bordering on 95. His motto seems to be to make each year an improvement over the preceding one. Besides being a brilliant student Greene is active in the social activities of the University. Greene will enter the Harvard Law School next fall. We are certain that he will make a brilliant record there and reflect credit to the University. We predict for him in the field of law a still greater success than his brilliant father made.

name: Henry Bradley Johnson, A.B.
motto: "Life is but a game of chance, but the probability of your chance lies within you."
description: Bradley, though small in stature, fills a large place in the hearts of his friends. Due to his kindness and pleasing personality his friends are many at the University—among the girls as well as the boys. Bradley came to us with the idea of becoming a southern planter of the old school, and therefore registered for agriculture. The long walks to Ag Hill, together with his scholarly inclination, were the deciding factors in his changing to the study of the liberal arts. He made a drastic change from Animal Husbandry to Greek, but has made a success. He has done creditable work in College and at the same time has been one of our outstanding social leaders. No dance or party of any consequence at Georgia would be complete without him. Bradley, we all wish you the best of happiness and success. With your rare personal magnetism you should have no difficulty in sailing the sea of life.

name: James Rufus Johnson, B.S.C.
motto: "To succeed you must be willing to pay the price of success which is work. Patient and perservering work is the key to success."
clubs: Delegate to National Student Volunteer Convention, '24
description: Genius is work; this is the secret of the success of "J. Rufus," for by hard work and always sticking to any job he has undertaken he has proven to his friends as well as to his University that he will make good in the future. Modesty, dignity, integrity of character, and the willingness to work for the things which he believes to be right have stamped him as a valuable man, causing both his friends and his college to sincerely respect him. He has financed his way through school by working in his spare moments, at the same time maintaining a high standard in his marks.

name: Rachael Luretia Keith, B.S.H.E.
motto: "If reasons were as plentiful as blackberries, I would give no man reason under compulsion."
clubs: Senior Representative on Executive Council of Homecon Club
description: Fortunate is the class of '25 to claim as its own a student possessing the ability and personality of Rachael. This is her first resident year at Georgia and she has attained an enviable record in her work. One might be led to think she spent all her time at her books, but we find her studies have not prevented her enjoying a prominent part in the student activities as well as the social side of college life. With all of these qualities and her strong determination, Rachael will be sure to succeed in any position she may endeavor to fill. And our wish for her is the opportunity to work out some of the ideas her creative mind has formulated. Here's to you, Rachael, may you find in life the realization of every ambition and friends who will appreciate you as do your college-mates at old Georgia.

name: Emily Mullikin LaBoon
description: Emily came to us in the fall of '21 from the "Classic City," but she divorced Georgia in 1922 and spent her Sophomore year at Chicora College. The call of her youthful alma mater was too strong, and 1923 found her again on familiar grounds among friendly faces. She has never thought of leaving again. To look at this demure young girl one would not think that behind the penetrating gaze lies a fertile and active brain charged with scientific knowledge, for Emily's natural propensities trail the light of science. She occasionally takes a peep into the "Psychic realm," besides possessing a well-known skill in the culinary arts. Add to the above skills and intelligence a charming personality, subtleness of humor, graciousness of manner, and you have Emily, who will be remembered always by those who know her best.

name: Lucius Lamar, Jr.
description: A little old town of southern Georgia, known to many as Dawson, boasts proudly of her well-loved and illustrious senior representative at the University of Georgia, Slick Lamar. Since his shadow first darkened the entrance arch to the University campus one bright September morn four years ago, Slick has grown and developed, surely and steadily, into a man of the first degree, loved by many and highly respected by all. Slick has distinguished himself by showing no mean ability in handling the business affairs of the University Glee Club; he has shown his popularity by being a member of various social clubs, notably the Senate and the Biftads Club.

name: Calvin Howard Langford
motto: "Keep fighting; when you are tiring your foe is tiring, too; the fight is never hopeless."
description: It's hard to tell about Langford. You would not understand him if I wrote about him, and you would not understand him if you met him. He is quiet and unassuming, but often breaking out in unexpected spots and scoring a perfect score. Langford has a prodigious mind, being able to hoard up a whole year's lectures in his head until examination time and then "shooting" his prof cold. In addition to the above mentioned qualities, Langford is quite an athlete, "Mexican" and otherwise. On the cinder path it is rumored that as soon as he is out of sight he puts on winged sandals and leaves his opponents far to the rear. We feel confident that Langford can't help but succeed in whatever he undertakes in the future.

name: Garnett Taylor Langford
motto: "Work hard and tie your bull on the outside."
description: Everyone who has seen this long, lean, lanky "drink of water" strolling down the street, sporting a derby and a cane with a nonchalance that would turn Lord Chesterfield green with envy, will never forget "Skinny." "Skinny" is another three-year man, hailing from the fair city of Royston. Under the guidance of the far-famed "pistol" he has fared rather well in the realm of banking, marketing, accounting and other phases of commercial work. So far as can be learned "Skinny" is immune from the attractions of the fairer sex. "Skinny's" friendly attitude and genial disposition have won for him countless friends and admirers, among whom any man in college would be proud to find his name. The best wishes of his many friends go with him now as we say, "Bon Voyage," Skinny.

name: Katherine D. Lanier
motto: "Putting off 'till tomorrow Will lead us to sorrow; Beginning today Is the very best way,"
description: Prior to her career as a University student, "Katie" had become well-known on the campus through her work as District Supervisor of Home Demonstration Work for the Savannah District and her attendance on Short Courses and Summer School. Believing that— "Putting off 'till tomorrow Will lead us to sorrow; Beginning today Is the very best way," she armed herself with a Physics text in the summer of 1923, and since has made it her daily companion and constant solace. Reluctantly she lays it aside. Exemplifying the adage that— "Things don't turn up in this world Until somebody turns them up: A pound of pluck is worth a ton of luck," she finds no difficulty insurmountable. "From all your classmates and friends, 'Katie,' here's to 'ye future.'"

name: Claud William Lowe
enrolltime: four years ago as a freshman
description: Claud "Whang" Lowe first saw light in the city of Buena Vista, Georgia. It is from this metropolis of southern Georgia that he entered the University four years ago as a freshman. In the one and three years of his college life Claud has demonstrated to the fullest extent that the old saying, "He can who thinks he can," is true in its every sense. Unlike most boys on first entering college, Claud knew that he was no "Sir Oracle," and with the idea in mind that "ignorance is the beginning of wisdom" he has racked up noteworthy honors. While ascending the topmost heights in mental and social development, he has developed a character that is as firm and unshaken as Gibraltar, and a personality that is most pleasing. Claud may rightfully be called a true Southern Gentleman. What more can be said?

name: John Bernard Maddox, A.B.
motto: "Be good and you will be lonesome."
description: From the quiescent walks of this modest young man's college career, three conspicuous features have arisen: 1, "judge not that ye be not judged;" 2, "a large, much abused, yet sympathetic swivel chair," and of course, the girl. "J. B.'s." theory of association is beautiful in form and effect. A reserved, occasionally frolicsome party, who knows his rights and exercises them only so far as they do not infringe the rights of others, he has established a substantial clientele of sworn friends. And if no good he can speak of his acquaintances, he says nothing of them at all. His calm gestures of rebuttal have been known to put to rout that peerless argufier, Bill Ray.

name: Sara Evelyn Maddux, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Smile and the world smiles with you."
description: Sara can be described in three words— "full o' pep," a cherry laugh, a winning personality, happy disposition—all of these qualities and many more can be attributed to Sara. Already in the limelight of affairs at Georgia, she became our scintillating star when she appeared in "Clarence," the play put on by the Thalian Dramatic Club, in '23. In the first place, she was an "honorary member" of that club. In the second place, she played the lead so well that her name will always be remembered in connection with the stage, along with Bernhardt, Farrar, and others. But was such "fame" necessary to assure popularity for her? I am not sure whether popularity begets fame or fame begets popularity, but it is certain that Sara will never be left out either way. She possesses an over-abundance of both.

name: Charlie Holmes Martin, Jr.
description: The hills of Gainesville have made a never-to-be-forgotten contribution in the person of Charlie Martin, Jr. An ability to enjoy himself at the proper time, coupled with as great an ability to work, have made Charlie a respected man both in the classroom and on the ballroom floor. A wizard in a good, old-style bull session, a teller of beautiful tales about strange happenings in his native mountains. Truly, Charlie has made many friends through his democratic spirit. He cannot but succeed after his school time is over if he but encounters the world with the same effervescent smile and ready wit which he has shown us here at Georgia. Let us quiet down now until Charles, III, comes to quail beneath the thunder of Tom Reed.

name: Elizabeth Amanda Meredith
description: When we think of Elizabeth, we think of all that is honorable, beautiful, and highest, for she is always willing to help others strive for better things. Those who know Elizabeth realize that she is a good sport and a brilliant student. She has been with us only two years, but she leaves swinging a key. Elizabeth is full of optimism and always has time for everything. Responsibility she carries without the slightest ruffling of her feathers.

name: Harry LaFayette Middlebrooks, B.S.
description: And now, ladies and gentlemen, we take pleasure in introducing Mr. Harry LaFayette Middlebrooks, of Eatonton, Georgia, a gentleman, a scholar, a ladies' man and an athlete. In other words, a most versatile and well-meant young man. Harry has distinguished himself in the University along many lines. But none of his feats of brilliance probably have been the sources to him of the same inspiring thrills which he has received out of his baseballistic endeavors. He has been the regular third-basement on the Bulldogs' diamond outfit for the past three years and during that time he has been without a peer in the southland when it came to guarding the hot corner. His fielding has been so scintillating and his hitting spectacular in every degree. In spite of his remarkable athletic achievements "Flash" is as modest a young man as he was when he matriculated. And we doubt if any man has any more friends in the University than our hero who, as we have hinted, is blessed with a wonderful personality.

name: Gloria Miller, Ph.G.
motto: "Few things are the better for words."
description: At some time or other in her life Gloria must have heard the old truism that it is never worth while to worry. Endowed by the gods with one of the sweetest dispositions we have ever seen, this bright faced little maiden does her full share in casting sunbeams through the shadows that occasionally fall around the rest of us who are more susceptible to attacks of melancholy than she. And surely it is no little thing to say that anyone has the gift of making the lives of others brighter and happier for having come in contact with her. Truly Gloria personifies Spring and Eternal Youth, for no matter how cold and dreary the day is, we see springing flowers and blue skies in her smiling face and hear singing birds and rippling brooks in her laughter. For her we are wishing all the best things that life has to offer.

name: Robert Meriwether Middleton
motto: "Manhood, not scholarship, is the first aim of education."
description: Bob did his high school work at Schultz High, North Augusta, and came to the University in the Fall of 1921 with the definite purpose in mind to get full benefits from opportunities offered here. His gentlemanly qualities, honesty, trustworthiness, sterling character, and host of friends, as well as his scholastic record show that he has achieved his purpose. Success in life is three-fourths perspiration and one-fourth inspiration. So it is thought that Middleton has seventy-five per cent of a chance for success; and we have a faint idea that he has a source for the twenty-five per cent of inspiration. For now, he has aspirations of reaching greater things than have been in his possession. So here's to your success in life, Bob, and may the reward you leave behind be as clean as the record you are leaving here at Georgia.

name: Christine Sophronia Moon
motto: "Always be true to the best that is in you."
enrolltime: entered the University as a Soph
description: Just an all-round girl, a loyal friend, a pal—that's Chris. Coming to the University from Bessie Tift, where, for a year, she had "walked away" with all athletic honors, she entered the University as a Soph, and for three years has, with her easy-going and jolly disposition and her ambition, won for herself the love and respect of pupils and teachers. Chris has a personality that makes and holds friends and is most loved and appreciated by those who know her most intimately. Among the many laudable characteristics which she possesses, sympathy, love of the honorable, the just, the right, and unselfish loyalty to her friends are outstanding. With this rare combination of character and the never-failing and contagious smile that have meant so much in the past, the limit of her future success can not be predicted.

name: Dorothy Jositte Moran
motto: "Wisdom, justice and moderation—these three, but the greatest of these is a sense of humor."
description: "With the wisdom of Solomon and the versatility of Franklin." What a weighty description for a college girl, and especially one with red hair and a gorgeous soprano voice. Dorothy came to Georgia three years ago when co-education was very young on the campus and in those three years she has been prominently connected with every phase of the organization and growth of woman's activities on the campus. Her interests and talents are varied and she has given her abilities to the literary, social, civic and artistic phases of college life and has still found time to make an enviable scholastic record. Dorothy's specialties are languages and music and the Girl's Glee Club owes its organization and growth to her able leadership. Dependable, a hard worker and a true friend she is the type of girl that the University will be proud to call a "Georgia Girl."

name: John Cooper Morcock, Jr.
motto: "If you are right, why give a whoop what others think."
clubs: Delegate to Indianapolis Student Volunteer Convention
description: Start a little noise, fellows, we can understand better. "Coop" has the floor. One gains the greatest impression of our hero while he is profoundly and loudly proclaiming his honest opinions before a club or society meeting. His elucidations are usually of a reformative nature. Though not in every case successful, he is nevertheless admired for his fighting spirit. "Coop" has succeeded in running the gamut of the four-year course in three. During these years he has divided his time into three parts: books, B. Y. P. U., and the fair sex, pursuing each diligently. He reaps a fair harvest in the first two fields; with time the third will ripen. Your friends follow you "Coop," and feel sure that the spirit that you have displayed while at college will grow better and larger with maturing years. We believe your ambitions and desires will be satisfied and that the greatest success and happiness will be yours.

name: Joseph Brown Mosteller
motto: "Give all thine ear but none thy tongue, for silence is a rare gem."
description: Monk, better known to his friends as J. B., came to the University four years ago and immediately decided to become a disciple of "Little Charlie." J. B. is a man of few words and his quiet and unassuming manners have won for him many friends, both male and female, during his sojourn here. Outside of several visits to the dean and various other officials he has had a very successful college career. Exposing himself to such a difficult course as that of civil engineering he has naturally had to betake himself more to his studies than he would have otherwise, nevertheless, he has found time for several hurried visits to the "co-ed barn." One of his noblest characteristics and greatest assets is his loyalty to his friends. We understand that, upon graduation, he is going to the south sea islands to practice his profession.

name: John Hulon Mote
clubs: Joe Brown Connally Scholarship in Georgia History
description: There are a lot of misfits in the world, there always has been, and in all probability, will be; all of which is probably due to a failure to apply one's talents in the proper manner and direction. But fellows who know Mote best will tell you that there can be no such error in his choice of a life occupation. He is conscientious, a hard worker, and if we are to judge by his college record, a man who will make his mark in the world of chemists. He is not the kind of fellow that we hate to see leave the University, but the kind that we like to see go out as a good representative to the world of the institution. Just an all-round good fellow about whom there is no "sham" or "pretend-to-be."

name: William Slater Murray, B.S.
motto: "By learning more one becomes wiser, By doing more one becomes greater. Learn more to do more."
description: And now, folks, we present for your approval William Slater Murray, better known to the student body as Bill. Bill entered the University in the fall of '21, coming to us from Augusta. He took Pre Med his first two years. Bill is a registered Pharmacist, and the call of the Drug Store is too great for him. He changed his course when beginning his third year at the University, and intends to follow the drug profession when he graduates this June. Bill's work has been very commendable during his four years with us as he did not have the prep advantages that most students have, and much honor is due him. We know you will make good, Bill, and the best of luck to you.

name: Irvin Philips Myerson, A.B.J.
motto: "Let me make the nation laugh and I care not who makes its laws."
