description: We speak of a writer as gifted. We call this or that orator versatile. The good student we call a scholar. We may describe a business man as a man of parts. What, then, can we say of a man to whom no one of these descriptions could be applied except with the others, for else he would not be adequately described? We know not what words to use or what he may be compared to, we merely know that Irvin P. Myerson is such a person. Yet he is no jack of all trades, neither is he just a dilettante, for to him the day's work is a noble task to perform, and he an artist doing a work of art while executing the task, be it pleasant or otherwise. Irvin, old boy, may the world be your studio, and your reward according to your worthy deserts.

name: Birdie McAllister
motto: "Real happiness comes only through the conscious pursuit of a worthy purpose."
description: "What kind of school would this school be If all its students were like you and me?" We could answer this question quite joyfully and willingly if we knew or even thought, that all the students were like Byrd. Besides taking many honors she has proved herself a good sport and the best of friends. Despite all that we have said, we'll have to admit that Byrd has one fault—she will argue. Whether or not that is a serious fault we will leave for you to decide, and a decision can best be made after you have heard one of her lengthy discourses on Zoo or some other favorite subject. "It's not because you're jolly, And never a trifle blue; It's not because your words Are never slow and few, But the reason we all love you Is just because you're you."

name: Martin Windsor McKee
motto: "Give to the world the best that you have and the best will come back to you."
description: "Mack," as he is familiarly known to his intimate friends, entered Georgia in the fall of 1924 having finished at a junior college back in the dim past. By attending summer school, for "Mack" is a teacher, he is able to get his degree in one year. Since coming to Georgia this steady young man has imbibed the "spirit" and is fully recognized by those who know him best, as one of the class of '25. The very name of this young gentleman is indicative of his steadiness and solidarity. A hard and persistent worker, with a sane attitude toward life coupled with integrity and strength of character. We predict for this young man success in his chosen field of endeavor, agriculture.

name: Lucius Blakemore McLendon, B.S.A.
motto: "Aim high for it isn't any harder on your gun to hit an eagle than to splinter a barn door."
description: Folks we submit for your approval the very best that Sasser has to offer. Gaze upon the countenance of the greatest county agent the College of Agriculture has ever aggrandized the rural population of the state with. Luke's agrarian nature from freshmanhood has submitted to a high state of advancement. A pugnacity to remain in New College and a too familiar association with friend "Bull" is our only dissatisfaction with his college career. A hard worker and a conscientious objector has made Luke's struggle easy and his presence anything but pleasant, so we send him from our midst. Go boy, but keep that versatile personality, you have won a place in the hearts of many, wherever we meet, your hand-clasp will be reminiscent of days both good and bad here where we met you.

name: James Kinard McNeill, B.S.
motto: "Think twice before you speak and then don't speak."
description: "Mac" completed the five-year preparatory course at Richmond Academy, making it possible for him to enter the sophomore class here. He is a man of many peculiarities, and to some he may appear cold and indifferent but to know him intimately is to appreciate him. Though making very creditable grades during his college career, "Mac" has found ample time to do a vast amount of general reading and general research. All have enjoyed immensely his cartoons and original witticisms appearing from time to time in university periodicals. "Mac" plans to return to the University of Georgia next year for a post-graduate course, and to extend his research work a bit further. Following this it is his intention to study art—taking the necessary training at a northern institute.

name: McRainey, Neal Duncan
description: This is the second McRainey to graduate from the University within the last two years. This is also the second McRainey to attain, during his sojourn in and about the halls of knowledge, the highest scholastic honors offered by this institution. "Mac" is a student in the truest sense of the word. He has a perception and keenness not often found, together with thorough adherence to the philosophy of work. However, he isn't an advocate of all work and no play. "Mac" is industrious, conscientious, persevering, and the possessor of a pronounced affability. With these desirable qualities, coupled with his high ideals, we feel quite confident that "Mac" will experience little difficulty in attaining that high degree of success he so justly deserves.

name: Nelson, Lucius Henry
motto: "It takes a little of it all—Work hard, Play hard, Fight hard and Love hard; yet remembering that other things succeed besides success."
clubs: University Delegate to Student Council Federation, '24
description: "Smooth water runs deep." Calmness, reserve, and a native ability to fathom the depths and circumvent the borders of the dark mysteries of agricultural subjects is characteristic of "Little Nelson." This epithet was bestowed upon him because of his Big Brother predecessor in the University. Concentration of his versatile talents upon the task at hand, and a degree of stick-a-bility possessed by few accounts for his enviable scholarly standing.

name: Evelyn O'Quinn
description: Rare is the soul which maintains its dignity and yet does not lose the common touch. Evelyn is genuine; she has a human interest in music and the arts; she has a human interest in human beings; and she has managed to keep a diary (Younger Generation that she is!) which is lacking only in French names, roguish monarchs, and maidens blushing behind broad fans. What more can a busy pharmacist be expected to do? And remember, too, that she has succeeded in everything she has turned her hand to . . . . . What do we predict for Evelyn? Nothing! Temperaments like hers defy prophecy. We can only wonder what she will do next.

name: Frederick William Orr
motto: "A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, and loving favor than silver or gold."
description: Fritz is an Athens product, and this Classic City should be proud to call him so. He entered the University four years ago and has made quite an enviable record. Not only is he termed a Chemistry shark, but a crack monkey driller, a good track man, and the possessor of a pleasing, outstanding personality. This, combined with his sterling character and ideals of the highest type make him a man the University and his friends can point to with much pride. Fritz, with the exception of a few trips to Alabama (!), has been a persistent worker these four years, taking part in every branch of college activity.

name: Auburn Ganbelle Owens, A.B.
motto: "Life is a practical joke of which we are the victims."
description: It was but three years ago that this rare specimen of homo sapiens, whose likeness you see pictured above, found himself among us after four years of wandering in the academic groves of Augusta. As he came, so he goes. Not with the blare of trumpets nor the advance notices of the press-agent was he ushered into our midst, but was among us some time before we realized that he was here. But realization of his presence soon brought appreciation of his many sterling qualities both as a student and a man. On the diamond as well as in the class room, he has conducted himself in the true Georgia manner, which is no more nor less than that of the sportsman and gentleman. He studied hard, he played hard. That he will add more lustre to the class of '25 during the years that lie before him in the work-a-day world of business, is the confident expectation of every one, so we all wish you bon voyage on your trip "across".

name: Donovan Owens, B.S.C.
motto: "If you put green glasses on a mule and feed him excelsior to make him think it is hay, he will die."
description: "Donnie" does not believe in taking life too seriously. He has done well along scholarship lines and has accomplished his purpose at the University in its fullest extent, but from all outward appearances he has never gone to the trouble to let anyone know that he was ever worried about what was to come next. His many friends will hate to see him leave because on each occasion he has been the veritable "Life of the Party." Not that he carries on, or becomes clownish, but rather because his personality seems to penetrate deeper and deeper from the time he makes his appearance until the time he effects his departure. Donnie, although the future does not seem to bother him too seriously, will make his mark in the world because he seems to get a good start on everything without any obvious effort. The honors he has gained at the University have been entirely meritorious, but at that he has deserved more.

name: George Colquit Dean, B.S.C.
description: George Dean can behave himself quite foolishly. Foolishly, that is, if it is foolish to defy anything that is powerfully established. If you are very stubborn, and very indifferent to other people's opinions—well, it is plain what will happen. But stubbornness and self-confidence are really, in another sense, determination and courage, which, everyone knows, are great and lofty qualities. They are not too great for this man's carrying. So often virtues of this kind will take passage only on very dun-colored and monotonous vessels. Now, for once, they travel on a ship none the less strong and swift, but one so brightly dressed out with humorous pennons that the heart is gladdened at the sight of it. There are other passengers here, then, also. Mere determination and courage would never have run up these gay ensigns. Kindness is aboard, and generosity, and imagination—it is indeed a large and distinguished party.

name: James Ruey Patrick, A.B.S.S.
clubs: 1st Lieutenant F. A. O. R. C.
description: Here is the happy medium. Too tolerant to be an out-and-out conservative and too broad minded to join the ranks of the Bolshevists. Sincere of purpose and honest in opinion, Pat (they call him Pat) is continuously occupied and conscientiously pursuant of culture. Pat came to us from the United States army which he entered during the late World War as a buck and left as a commissioned officer. His stay at the University has been featured by the same graphical rise. He has done so well in Psychology that he he been given opportunities to teach others. He was elected to the Student Council without knowing he was a candidate until the election was over. Pat does not see life so much as a circus as many others of us do but he can appreciate humor when it shows thought. His methods will win him a niche of his own.

name: Alice Winn Peeples
motto: "To fill the hour—that is happiness; to fill the hour, and leave no crevice for a repentance or an approval."
description: Alice Winn is a rare specimen. She has never let studying interfere with her education and yet her scholastic record at Georgia is one of which any Phi Beta Kappa might well be proud. She has a jolly disposition that allows no dull moments and a sweetness of manner that captivates at once. These excellent attributes of character account for the hosts of boys and girls at Georgia who delight in numbering her among their best and truest friends. Another good thing about Alice Winn is that she is never rattled. No matter what happens, the world will never know but that all is well with her. Because of this last quality we feel sure that she will be successful in whatever she attempts to do. The best wish that we can make for her is that she will find in after life as many loyal friends as she has won for herself during her four years at Georgia.

name: John Heidt Pendergrast
description: In these days of hurry and scuffle it is delightful to find a man who maintains a composure that is startling, who looks at everything with a philosophic calm, and who believes that wise leisure holds the secret of what small bit of worldly success and happiness may come to us poor mortals. This man can raise a lusty shout at a close victory. He can discuss Shakesperian drama and sing Mendelssohn oratorios. He can hold his own with bootleggers and princes. This man will give a lot to the world if the world will only heed him; and the world will be a fool if it fails to heed him.

name: Evelyn Perry
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you."
enrolltime: After finishing at G. S. C. W. in 1923
description: After finishing at G. S. C. W. in 1923, Evelyn came to Georgia to add a few more collections to her abundant store of knowledge. To be conventional one should start with "she is sweet; she is attractive; she is beautiful"—but even conventionality fails to express the true worth of our Evelyn. Sincere and true, loyal and kind, she is a friend indeed and whether at work or play her alertness, originality and intellectual powers combine to make of her "a good student." Her high ideals, her sweet Christian character, and her splendid disposition have endeared her to so many that instead of predicting and wishing for her a great future (which would necessarily take her away) they arise with one accord and say "Give us Evelyn as she is."

name: Henry Waller Powers
description: "Beef" Powers is a product of Rome, Ga., and has represented his home town in a way that one may justly commend. He is slow to praise a friend and never condemns an associate. He is never flashy or over enthusiastic, but he possesses a steady wire for carrying out his purpose. He took Agriculture because of his real and unaffected love for Nature. It seems as though Keats has converted him to the belief that "A thing of beauty is a joy forever." At any rate he spends a good bit of his time with the fairer students of the University. He certainly lives up to his motto, "Never Worry," or if he has trouble he keeps that to himself. His pleasant smile, consideration for others and sterling character have won many friends who are proud to have him go out into the world as a representative by which the University may be judged.

name: Foy Clifford Randall, D.V.M.
motto: "To fight to the end—if perchance beaten, admit it, and try again."
description: In Foy you see a man determined and bound to overcome his handicaps in making a success in life. Foy entered the University of Georgia in the Fall of 1921 and with a very limited prep. school education came forward, made up all entrance units and has steadily forged ahead each year making better marks than before. He entered Military Service during the World War and was injured in performing his duty, but now due to his perseverance has risen from the ranks so to speak, and due to his love for the Veterinary Profession will make a marked success in the practice of Veterinary Medicine. As to his motto; he is made of the stuff which causes men to fight to the end but his sense of fairness and upright dealing will cause him to admit it and then try again. Success to you Foy and next time heard from let us hope that you will have earned all that your faithful heart deserves and desires.

name: Mary Lucille Turner, B.S.H.E.
motto: "’Twas her thinking of others made you think of her."
description: She is quiet, modest, retiring, absolutely dependable, and she possesses a mind that works with watch-like precision. Add to that a most unexpected and sparkling sense of humor and you have Lucille. As Home Demonstration Agent in Richmond County for two years and then as District Supervisor of eighteen counties in the Atlanta District she has shown that we need not fear the outcome of any effort directed by her. She has already wrested success from the hand of Fate. But knowing that Knowledge is power she came to Georgia, her Alma Mater, determined to enlarge her opportunity for service. She is a hard and consistent worker, a scholar in the true sense of the word, a true and loyal friend. She is emblematic of all that is best and highest in womanhood. We wish her happiness in her work and the fulfillment of her fondest dreams.

name: Paul Nettleton Richards
motto: "Be good at doing a number of things, but select at least one thing and excel in it."
description: Take a look at the blond Hercules above; good-natured and a regular fellow, but one of the neatest packages of muscles ever put up by Mother Nature. He is an Athens boy and she may well be proud of him, as he does not stop at being a strong man in body but is also a stellar in his class work, and, true to all good ring rules, he never gives up. After once tackling a job, he stays until he puts both its shoulders on the mat. So, girls, as you have heard the simple facts of the case, you had better use strategy in capturing this "baby," for he is an artist with dumb-bells. We have come to the parting of the way and we hate to lose him, but as the world needs men who do not quit, we must bid him adieu, and wish him the best of luck.

name: Marcella Richardson
motto: "It's the things we always hold That we will lose some day; The only things we ever keep Are what we give away."
description: A jollier disposition and sweeter manners can not be found. A smile and some witty saying is always ready to greet you, even when she has studied half the night over some pharmacy problem. It is very hard indeed to picture this demure little blond concocting some detailed prescription in a drug store. Far more easy is it to picture her concocting dainty dishes in a petite kitchenette—and it is known that she is rather good on prescriptions for "heart troubles." At any rate we wish you the best of luck wherever you are, whether it be meting out drugs or food supplies, here's to you, Marcella.

name: Robert James Richardson, B.S.A.
motto: "The art of living is to know when to stop."
description: The handsome gentleman above, familiarly known as "Bob," hailed originally from Ohio, the Buckeye State, but he is a real Georgia Cracker now. From this good-natured chap the University of Georgia didn't know what to expect, but he has proven himself a genius in everything he undertook. Besides standing out prominently in his scholastic work, he has shown splendid qualities of leadership, and leaves an enviable athletic record behind him. "Bob" has two failings—women and horses, and he is, as some doubtless know, a past-master in the art of handling both. We will all miss Bob and hope that he will succeed in all his undertakings, but we seriously advise against trying to catch a pig in a ditch; so we are bidding him adieu, and wishing him the best of luck.

name: Herbert Bernard Rothschild, B.S.
motto: "A man's first care should be to avoid the reproaches of his own heart."
description: Herbert started off in College with the standard of a student and he has not failed to uphold that standard for four years. All with whom he comes in contact are aware of his faithful and steady pursuit of knowledge; his accumulation of accurate facts along with an uncanny logical reasoning power, make him the counsellor of his many friends. Herbert started in 1921, preparing himself for entrance into a medical college, and after he leaves fair Georgia, medical books galore will be his companions. His heart, mind and sinew are bent on his work and success must be the outcome of his unweary efforts.

name: Dorothy Rowland
description: Dot is small of stature, yet she makes up in quality for what she lacks in quantity. Happy-go-lucky and apparently as care-free as a lark, she has the optimistic philosophy that everything will turn out right—and it always does. She is as witty as can be, and regardless of time or place, she is always the life of the party. A living example of being able to do two things at once, for during the past two years while studying at Georgia, she has been teaching Home Economics at Lucy Cobb Institute. In consideration of these facts her friends predict a bright future for her.

name: Dwight Warren Ryther, Jr.
motto: "A worm is the only thing that cannot stumble."
description: He came to Georgia from Fort Leavenworth, having spent most of his life wandering from pillar to post. Yet we feel that at heart he is a loyal Georgian. Since being here he has done especially well in Secretarial and Military fields. His shoulders are now adorned by "three buttons." He also boasts a couple of medals for hurdling and is out to add more to his collection this year. Over in the Secretarial Department he is trying to establish the "Hunt and Pick" method of typewriting, and the "Longest" method of shorthand. Maybe his Yankee business ability will pull through these two new innovations of the secretarial world.

name: Roy Thomas Scoggins
motto: "Look out for yourself as the other man will not look out for you."
clubs: Cavalry Unit, R. O. T. C.
description: After finishing the Athens High School, Roy T. decided to finish his learning by entering his State University. He can be seen most every morning hurrying down Prince Avenue in his fliver trying to get to an eight twenty-five class. During his four years at this University he has won countless numbers of friends and accomplished a great deal by hard work. His quiet, unassuming manner and his very admirable habit of never flaunting to the world his many virtues has placed him high in the esteem of all that know him. His pleasing personality, which enables him to make friends and hold them, and his ability to deliver the goods in a first-rate manner, lead us to predict for him a most successful career.

name: Walter Edwin Sewell
motto: "Even though the task is small, do it with all thy might, so are the greatest deeds accomplished."
clubs: Captain-Adjutant, R. O. T. C. Infantry, '24-'25
description: In the four years "Doc" has been at Georgia he has acquired his A.B. with highest honors possible and has also finished all but the last round for a B.S. Engineering degree with a similar record. He has won fame in two different fields of study while most of us strive to pass in one. He has proven a military man of exceptional merit, a genius at solving problems and possesses a social record that is most enviable. How he has found time to take part in so many college activities and still maintain his most excellent average is a mystery to the rest of us. Besides doing all this "Doc" has many real friends who are confident about his future, because "Doc" is of the material from which success is made.

name: Herschel Henry Shores
motto: "Speech was given to man to conceal his thoughts."
description: Shores came to us four years ago under the auspices of the "Rome Society of Scientific Research Endeavors." His time in the College of Agriculture has been used by this society as an experimentation in being positive culmination to the Chinese query that, "If a man is lost shall he find himself again?" It is most edifying to state that Shores has wonderfully found himself. This success we attribute to the instrumentality of quite a few summer schools which he has attended. It is now up to you Herschel Henry to present yourself amicably to the people of the state in your field of endeavor. We know nought of an instance in Shore's life here but that which reflects commendation of his affability. His friendship we esteem with honor. Pleasantly we meet again Herschel.

name: Frank Joseph Slater
motto: "Care not for what might have been, what is to be, is pertinent."
description: Frank set sail from "Georgia's Port" in the fall of twenty-one chartered for the sea of scientific knowledge and after a four years' cruise has arrived at the harbor of "Dip." A Lilliputian of stature and a giant of intellect. In the years a new niche will be cut in the salon of fame and though the excavation need not be extensive it will be of equal import with the greatest. For our expectations will be sorely disappointed if Frank does not fill the niche. Frank's personality would be creditable to a Galahad, and it has won for him the admiration and friendship of all those who have had the pleasure of coming in contact with him. Such personalities as his shall live foremost in our memories of college life. May you live at the big end of the cornucopia Frank, and may the cornucopia be filled with happiness.

name: Garnet Fain Slaughter
motto: "Some are born great, some have greatness thrust upon them, but I have achieved greatness."
clubs: Cadet Colonel R. O. T. C., '25
description: This genial youth hails from the Classic City and it is justly proud of its "Doc"—and he's strong for it too. "Doc" is a man we are proud to call our friend. Since his very first day at Georgia his infectious good nature got us and we all stuck to him. Many and varied are the things "Doc" has done during his stay at the University. He has shown his ability and interest in all phases of college life, possessing indefatigable determination. "Doc" is one of the kind of men to whom you can spill all your troubles and woes, and then go off feeling better, for he is a quiet fellow, giving you the chance to say it yourself—a true mate. Probing his past reveals not a single dark spot. We predict for "Doc" a future full to overflowing in anything he attempts.

name: Robert Harris Smalley
motto: "Friends that must be bought should not be sought."
description: Having an ever reverent attitude toward such things as domestic animals, plants and other things R. H. found his way into the State College of Agriculture of the University. His relations with a well known biblical syndicate has taken him into remote parts of many of the Atlantic states and has made of him a well traveled man. Remuneration from this source permits this auspicious personage retirement at his own discretion. But far be it from him to allow riches to spoil his brilliant future career at this youthful period of his existence. As a country school teacher we predict him a marvelous success. Here is a man to set the woods afire, so with our utter confidence in him we hope to see great things materialize at his hands.

name: Hermon Burton Smith, D.V.M.
motto: "The Lord helps those who help themselves. Build on a broad foundation and your time will come."
clubs: Department Representative of the Agricultural Quarterly
Commander of the Joe Brown Connally Chapter No: 2 Disabled American Veterans of the World War, July 5, 1924, to January 5, 1925
description: It is a pleasure to write about a man with the character, ideals, energy and ability that this man has. After serving his country in the World's War and also on the Mexican border, Smith came to the University so as to become better equipped to serve his country in civilian life. He has made an excellent record here completing the D. V. M. degree in the required four years, and getting credit for several extra hours' work at the same time. Always in a hurry is the way you will find him, but never too busy to speak a cheerful word to his friends, which are many, both among the students and the people of Athens.

name: Hershel Eugene Smith, A.B.
motto: "Honor is the greatest asset that a man can possess."
enrolltime: In his three years with us
description: In his three years with us, this young Apollo has distinguished himself in ways becoming a man possessing such rare qualities as are exemplified in him. A thoroughbred through and through. "His Majesty" well deserves seriously the title that is humorously given him, for he truly excites the admiration and respect of his many friends and acquaintances. His simple, unassuming, but polished manner, backed by that good practical sense which is so often lacking in most of us, has distinguished him in his various activities at the University. In expecting great things from one possessing such ambition, ability, intelligence, and grandeur of character, we could hardly be disappointed.

name: John Sherman Smith, B.S.A.
high school: Eighth District A. & M. High School
motto: "Aim at higher things in life; greater, nobler and sweeter, go for them, they will surely not come to you."
description: John Sherman Smith represents his home town well. He finished the Eighth District A. & M. High School in 1917, and intended entering the University of Georgia, but the World War broke into his plans. He enlisted in 1917 and saw eighteen months of overseas service. Upon returning to carry out his plans he entered the University of Georgia in the fall of 1922 with twenty-seven dollars and fifty cents in his pocket. Since then he has paid his expenses and done his class work too, finishing a four-year course in three years with two summer schools. Sherman, as he is known on the campus, is a steady and hard working boy. He possesses marked ability, a wonderful disposition, and highest morals of the truest type. Even with a degree in his hands, Sherman is not satisfied for he has aspirations of attaining greater heights upon the ladder of success.

name: William Hoke Southern, B.S.A.
motto: "First, be sure you are right, then steer straight ahead."
description: William Hoke Southern, Bill as we know him, came to the University of Georgia, February 24, 1921, with only two years of high school of which he had some eight years previous. By September 15, registering day, Bill had completed two years of high school work and was ready to enter freshman class. Four years more and he has a B.S.A. degree, the result of hard, constant, and conscientious work. Bill saw fourteen months of oversea service. He took part in the battles of the Marne Defensive, Marne Offensive, St. Mihiel and Argonne. In these major operations he was covered with mud and glory. He will carry on in the future as of the past. This is shown by the qualities that he possesses. He is temperate and true, a devout church worker. So Bill, we hope you will be successful in the great undertakings of your life.

name: Morris Layton Stokes
motto: "To enjoy life, and to help others enjoy it, without doing anyone harm; herein lies the whole of morality."
description: Intellectually and spiritually, this man is far in advance of his troublous times; socially, he belongs to the remote silver age of Louis XIV when the women were fair and hospitable and the men handsome and gracious. Hawkeye is a gentlemen to the manor born—one of those rare souls who ought to have been born with a million dollars. But, lacking the million, he has learned to work, and a great part of his effort has been exerted toward satisfying his consuming intellectual curiosity. He is cultured—cultured in the sense in which Matthew Arnold used the word. He plays and he works; and he loves women and books.

name: Mary Strahan
description: To those who know her nothing need be said. Those who have never had the pleasure of becoming acquainted with her could learn much from observing this determined and steady worker. Her long list of honors is an indication of the high esteem in which she is held by her fellow students. Mary's record stands as one of the most brilliant in recent years; in fact, the figures sound more like a dollar's worth of German marks than a Senior's average. To one so worthy success will always come. May you, Mary, gain all the finer things of life, and ever be crowned with happiness.

name: Sarah Tarragano
motto: "Nothing is either good or bad, but thinking makes it so."
description: And now comes Sarah. Four years ago, this breath from the old world entered our portals soon to be famed as one who could tread with airy grace the most intricate step of the dance; and who wore with jaunty ease the most becoming of dress. Today, she goes forth from her Alma Mater, one whose nimble fingers can fashion with equal ease creations that smack of gay Paree, or fairy concoctions, which vie only with her bright eyes and cheery laughter in the tempting of mortal man. Those dancing feet have learned to follow the path of duty; and thus she has been able to develop her many talents. Yet, who has ever known Sarah as an ardent burner of the midnight oil or an undue sacrifice of pleasure? Truly she is one whom the gods have seen fit to smile upon, and may the fates continue to weave the threads of her life in the gayest of patterns.

name: Jefferson Wright Tatum
motto: "Openmindedness is the basic element for the solution of any of life's problems."
clubs: First Lieutenant R. O. T. C.
description: Four years ago J. W. moored his bateau on the banks of the Chattahoochee, left the city of Ft. Gaines behind, and came to the University in order that he might become more efficiently equipped for the journey on this rugged sea of life. A winning personality of the highest degree, along with a witty statement to suit every occasion has made friends for him by the scores among his fellow students and all with whom he has come in contact, at the same time attaining knowledge has been his primary objective. Listening a lot and carefully weighing every statement heard before accepting it as true, and always seeking for new information is characteristic of him. These traits along with being a gentleman in every true sense of the word makes J. W. a man who will reflect credit upon his Alma Mater in later years. The best of luck to you is our sincere wish.

name: Sarah Tarragano
motto: "Nothing is either good or bad, but thinking makes it so."

Four years ago, this breath from the old world entered our portals soon to be famed as one who could tread with airy grace the most intricate step of the dance; and who wore with jaunty ease the most becoming of dress. motto: "Openmindedness is the basic element for the solution of any of life's problems."

name: N. James Taylor, D.V.M.
clubs: All-Southern selection for mythical football squad
description: Known to the campus and Athens as "Jim," All-Southern selection for mythical football squad, this man is rather conscientious in forgetting the honors showered upon him in a sincere appreciation for his cultural progress and the achievement of attaining his D. V. M. degree. When a man is given the prominence that has been given Jim in a specific sphere, it is often quite forgotten that this person has other interests in which his work may be equally as meritorious. From the viewpoint which must be held in a just consideration of the future, it may be said that Jim has done well in choosing other than the flame of temporary honor. Jim Taylor has established his name on the pages of football history and the world will remember, but Jim will forget because now he is starting out to give his name to humanity for humane administration.

name: William Walter Taylor, B.S.F.
motto: "Today is the tomorrow I worried about yesterday."
description: When "Buck" came to college in 1921, the fellows in his class very soon realized that here was a man to follow. He has done splendid work in his classes and has made many honors. He has gone a long way on the road to success in the record that he has made here. In the class room, in the Forestry Camp, at work, or at play, Buck has always been the same friendly, hard working, optimistic student. His many friends in college and in the city will miss him. However we know that wherever he is he will have many friends and that whatever he does, he will do well.

name: James David Thomason
motto: "Success is but a boomerang of faithful and unselfish labor."
description: Bayard Taylor's famous proverb, "The Bravest are the Gentlest," certainly can be applied to the above gentleman, who for four years has been a scintillating and spectacular figure in Georgia athletics. His playing has always been characterized with the same courageousness under fire which made Stonewall Jackson immortal. Yet off the field it is a known fact that Tommy is one of the gentlest and most modest men who ever played under red and black colors. Thomason has much of which to be proud. He performed on the gridiron for three years and has captured the coveted letter in baseball four times. In his senior year he captained the diamond purveyors. His gridiron work was brilliant and his baseball record probably has never been equaled by a Georgia man. He easily won places on the all-southern selections.

name: Thomas McKey Tillman
motto: "Let me be up and doing with a heart for any fate; still achieving, still pursuing, learn to labor and to wait."
description: Tom is a man one enjoys being with; his personality is so admirable that, like a chameleon, it blends perfectly with its immediate associates. Through years of association with him, one finds that as a friend he is always dependable; that he has the courage to assert his opinions and back his convictions. His nature is fundamentally pleasure-loving, but it is so curbed by an active conscience and an ingrained industry as to make him a nearly ideal "good fellow" and man. Tom has done very good work in his studies, though there is some rumor that his marks are due to "bootlicks." He has entered only a few branches of college activities but he has been a big man in these. Since Tom has an interest in finances and figures (all kinds), he will probably be a success in the banking business which he intends to enter.

name: Clovis Turk
motto: "Take nothing for granted, keep your eyes open and your aims high."
description: In Turk is found one of our most reliable men of the class of '25. He is the type of person who doesn't live for the glory of a high grade on his report, but is quite satisfied with a passing grade, provided he gets out of the course what he puts in it. In other words, Turk is one of those who believe that grades are nothing more than mere approximations of one's ability. Although, having no political aspirations, Turk has taken an interest in college activities, debates and publications in which he was justly rewarded with several places of distinction. In his quiet, amicable manner, Turk has gathered to his side a host of admiring friends who wish for him, in the years to come, the best that life has to offer.

name: Dewitt Campbell Turner
motto: "I care not for women; they can care for themselves."
description: THERE has been a lot of RUMOR about the country in YEARS of late to the effect THAT the Commercial World is RAPIDLY filling up. BUT we have never lost FAITH in the old adage THAT runs like this, "THERE is always room at THE top." AND somehow we know FROM observation that in after YEARS we can find above THE flotsam of life a FIGURE climbing the ladder, AND when we take a close-up WE shall recognize DeWITT.

name: Oscar Woody
motto: "Work is Nature's cure for all ills, be they great or small."
description: "Work is Nature's cure for all ills, be they great or small" is the philosophy upon which Oscar Woody has built his life. During the world war, he served in the Navy. Following this unpleasantness, he became a rehabilitation student in the University of Georgia and in one year gathered sufficient entrance units to start his college career. His average grade is above 87, placing him distinctly above the average in mentality and attainment. Although most of his time was devoted to scholastic work, he won a place on the College Live Stock Judging Team in 1923, and was also awarded the Junior Scholarship for the most proficient student in his class in 1924.

name: Sue Reid Vason
motto: "Let me build a house by the side of the road, and be a friend to man."
description: An old Lucy Cobb girl. Oh yes, of course—delicate, quiet, skilled in all domestic arts, demure, old fashioned—good gracious NO! Sue is hardly any of these; on a tennis court you can't down her, in a swimming pool she's hard to duck, at basketball you can't keep up with her. In other words Sue shines when there is anything in athletics going on. However she doesn't do so much in the studying line, but she is just naturally "good" enough to come through all right. Maybe it's her wit because she has an ample supply, that pulls her through. Her most striking characteristics are her frankness and absolute sincerity, two qualities which will always make her admired and counted among the "chosen few" wherever she goes; her friendship for those fortunate ones she likes knows no bounds. We love Sue and feel that she is really one of the best sports we have ever known.

name: William Horace Veale, B.S.C.
motto: "Real happiness comes from the conscious pursuit of a worthy purpose."
description: This distinguished looking blonde hails from the municipality of Watkinsville. Everyone knows that Athens is near Watkinsville. Four years ago this lad lighted in our "Classic City" to complete his education at his State University. At that time little did he realize he would play an important role in representing his Alma Mater on the cinder path. For the past three seasons "Pouli" has been a mainstay on our track team. If through life you demonstrate the same ever-striving strides that you have exercised throughout your college years your future is unlimited. Luck to you, "Pouli."

name: Thomas Hillyer Whitehead, B.S.
description: For three years no one could have thought that Tom's mind was concerned with anything but school and studies but now—well, he has become quite forgetful and has grudgingly accepted the nickname of "Snake". The reason? Oh! That's a sweet small person crowned with blonde hair whom we all know. Seriously, though, we all think Tom is a veritable prince of a good fellow, always willing to lend a hand at anything and one who is deserving of all the credit and success life can give him.

name: Charles Frederick Wiehrs, Jr.
description: After graduating at the head of his class in his home town this mighty young Savannahian entered the state university. As his enviable, paramount, multitude of laurels will indicate, he has seen service while in college. There have been few men to leave their respective Alma Maters who were able to represent their school in more different phases of activities than this individual. Charlie worked himself to the head of the cheer leader's staff; the top of the military division; and captained his mates on the basketball court last season and he proved himself to be one of the cleverest leaders that ever graced a Georgia basketball court.

name: Annie Laurie Wier
motto: "It's the way you set your sail that determines the way you go."
description: If you hear a great noise just after the clock has struck, you can rest at ease, for it is none other than Annie Laurie and her Ford. Always in a great hurry herself, she keeps her car panting—a student government conference here, a Thalian practice there, a fraternity meeting some place else, and studying, goodness only knows where. In spite of this rather careless and harum-scarum air, the achievements show that the bustle is not for nothing. Perhaps Annie Laurie attributes her success to her ability to ride her opponents down and overcome them in conversational duels. If the opponent is a man he is disarmed at once by her smile that begins with her eyes and ends with her lips. Whatever her methods, her popularity has justified them and we wish for those who do not know her a chance, that's all.

name: James Hunter Wilson, B.S.A.
motto: "Do right and fear no man; Don't write and fear no woman."
clubs: Asst. Business Mgr. of the Agriculturist, 1925
description: J. Hunter first made his appearance on the campus in the fall of '20, but for some reason, probably because he thought the knowledge offered here not equal to the requirements of a man of his caliber, he has since attended nearly every college from Georgia to Texas and as a result has secured facts concerning practically any and all subjects. In addition to his college work he has found time, by practical experience in the form of disseminating well-known books and other reading matter to the doors of readers far and near, to become a master salesman. But with all the penitence of the Prodigal Son, he returned to us in '23 with renewed enthusiasm and a desire to become an obedient subject. He has displayed a fine example of grim determination to win regardless of circumstances and we know he always will.

name: Francis Marion Young, B.S.Ag.
motto: "Those who think most, govern those who toil."
description: "Brigum" hails from the hills of North Georgia, where he early realized the need of a higher education. He made his preparations for college at Berry School, which is near Rome. He is a man that early came to the conclusion that his education should not interfere with his pleasures, but at the same time he has made a good record in his scholastic work. "Brigum" holds the record of being the only man in college who has held down two regular jobs, owned a Ford, won the distinction of being a ladies' man, made friends with everyone, and passed his work; he has worked regularly in the "beanery" as well as in the Ga. Co-op. since he entered college. If he makes as good in his profession as he has in his college career the University will have done another good deed. His many friends wish him a great success in his chosen work, which is the field of Agriculture.

name: Frederick Charles Drexel, B.S.A.
motto: "Goodness to goodness tendeth, goodness done is evil ended."
description: Drex concludes the fourth Tifton importation from his home to pass in and out of the portals of the University and take away letters of consummation from the College of Agriculture. Small in stature, amicable in nature, passive and demure in disposition, infrequent of speech and homely in mien sets you in the right trend with our meager descriptorial powers of him. An obligation he effaces in due time, his word is constancy, and filled with a philanthropic vigor. Patience is his. Drex came to us much as any other democratic Georgian, filling the role of freshman well, verdant, hopeful and innocent. The proper and logical metamorphose was exacted as of all who reach the finale. We now give him to you as a product of our instillation and associations. Take him and use him well. Drex feels he goes much wiser than he came. His originality will be an agricultural influence and impetus in his career.

name: Walter Ralph Reeves, Ph.G.
motto: "Be content, in work, to do the thing you can not presume because it's little."
description: In answer to the call of pharmacy, Ralph came over from South Carolina to register as one of us in the fall of '23. Though his stay here has covered a span of but two years, his memory will ever be green among us and we sincerely hope that the span of time over which we may call him friend will be long indeed. Ralph has made the most of his stay and has taken unto himself a Ph.G. He is only one of the many who have received the degree here but the best of the long lineage need have no scruples at receiving Walter Ralph Reeves to the round table.

name: Rena Katherine Davenport
motto: "To be, not to seem; to do not to dream."
description: A cheery smile, a sympathetic understanding, a lovable disposition; in toto, a character rare and beautiful—this is our Rena. Two years ago she came to us from G. S. C. W. and yet—it seems as always she has been ours. Her influence, which has been under the banner, "Right, not Might", has spread over acquaintances as well as friends and it is with a longing regret that we give her up. But in relinquishing our claim we bestow upon the children of our state a jewel in disguise of "Teacher". They will love her, we know. To you, Rena, we bid adieu from our campus, but not from our hearts. Our parting wish for you is a happy and successful voyage upon the great and mighty sea, Teaching!

name: Edwards Watkins Edwards, Jr.
motto: "It is not who won or lost, but how you played the game."
description: This noble character is commonly known as "Kid". He is from the mountain city of Ellijay, the metropolis of Gilmer County, Georgia. "Kid" has more Alma Maters than a dog has fleas. Graduating from Gordon institute in 1920, he entered Dahlonega in the fall of that year. After attending that institution for two years he decided to make a change, so in the fall of 1922 he entered Oglethorpe University. He withdrew at the end of the first term to go into business. Having grown tired of his commercial pursuits he entered our noble institution in the fall of 1923. He has attained success in the field of journalism, having been on the staff of the Red and Black. He is a lieutenant of infantry and will receive his commission in the reserves. "Kid" is planning on taking a law course at Harvard. Since he has great forensic abilities, he will most likely be a great politician some day.

name: Cobb, Andrew J.
description: As this long train of life sweeps into the golden distance to eternity carrying with it the souls of our honored dead, we are left awe-stricken as we gaze into the drear twilight of realities. Words are oft times poor instruments of expression as we attempt to pay homage to our great leaders who have passed on into the vast, unexplored land of dreams, so far and yet so near. Is it not, therefore, fitting that we, who have lived and worked under the light of this man of matchless modesty and refinement should pay tribute to his character, so full of majestic tenderness and wearing the white flower of a blameless life, and leave to history the praise of his achievements. It is with unstudied and unforced feeling that we, who feel ourselves richer for his having lived, do bow our heads and hearts in solemn tribute to his noble and generous life so worthy of admiration. Let it, therefore, be resolved that the life of this man, who, in the mild and mellow maturity of age, awaiting his call to that eternal silence, cast a noble light over our lives, shall not have been in vain, but shall sing itself forever into our memories, and forever echo in our hearts. Therefore, we, the Students of the Lumpkin Law School, do pay our humble respects to the memory of our beloved leader, Judge Andrew J. Cobb.

name: LeRoy Allen
motto: "Let not your handicap decrease your efforts in the game."
enrolltime: During his four years in college
description: "Chubby" is indeed the only nickname that could be associated with such a genial fellow. If you connect the physical quality of weight with the attributes of ability and good humor then you have an insight into his character. During his four years in college he has become a well-known figure in our University life. Honors have come his way and well they might, for such popularity as he has enjoyed comes only to the most deserving. The people of the state know him too, for they have met him on the Glee Club trips, laughed at his funny stories, and applauded his clever songs.

name: Frank DeVant Bozarth, Jr.
motto: "The Bird of Time has but a little way to flutter—and the Bird is on the Wing."
description: Jazz, an aspirant to the ancient and honorable profession of Blackstone and Marshal, came to us from Williamsburg, Virginia, via Darien High School, Darien, Georgia. There are those of your acquaintances who pride themselves on being "Radicals" and like nothing better than an opportunity to expound their, or more likely someone else's hair-raising opinions or theories. Well, Ladies and Gentlemen, here we have a "Broad-minded Conservative", as he takes pride in calling himself, who would rather argue in favor of the "happy medium" than eat, and eating means a lot in this young man's life. So far this "Legal Light" has not flared or played a spectacular part in the game of life but he has so diligently studied that great game from the side lines that we are confident that when he steps forth next June and starts his "drive" it will not be stopped until he has reached his goal in life.

name: Gussie Brooks
motto: "Nothing is bad but that it could be worse, nothing is good but that it could be better."
description: Gaze on the above if you would behold both beauty and brain, and mix with these two most desirable attributes a personality vivacious and charming. It is no need to say that this pleasing miss is none else than Gussie. Gussie chose to enter the field of law and her ability to follow a path so stern is unquestionable as is easily demonstrated by her splendid voice—so capable of pleading, yet stern and strong in its convictions; her clever and witty brain—that more than once outwitted her opponent in the vast halls of the Moot Court; and finally for her charming personality, which will play a strong part in winning any "twelve men, good and true" (and women, too). The best in the shop we give to you, Gussie. May life hold for you its best, its noblest and its loveliest.

name: Daniel Green Bryant
motto: "Tell not that you know not, but tell that which you know and use it to the greatest advantage."
clubs: Solicitor-General of University Moot Court for Third Term
Adjutant of D. A. V. of W. W.
description: This gentleman from Randolph County is a real Georgian, a true Southerner and a loyal American. Before entering the University in 1922, along with other things, he was a prominent teacher of singing and choirmaster; he has been active in musical circles in Athens. He served with the A. E. F. in France during the World War; such service will never be forgotten. "Bryant" is a gentleman in the truest sense, and is one of those individuals whose conduct is guided by well-regulated standards. He possesses a determination that is destined to overcome obstacles.

name: Robert Lee Pitman Carter, LL.B.
description: IN almost all the NEWSPAPERS that one picks up NOWADAYS there are stories OF SOME wicked asinine DEED which some criminal HAS done to an innocent citizen, AND invariably the SEWING circles and the MISSIONARY societies are SEEKING for him a pardon. UNTIL now the country wants SOMEONE to make a defense for US GUYS who don't go around CHOPPING people's heads off with axes. A PLEA for Justice. We believe that we CAN see such a one in "R. L. P."

name: Thomas Calvin Denmark, LL.B.
motto: "To do all by work, live my life so that it shall neither require defense nor apology."
description: Thomas Calvin, among one of the oldest students in the class, finished High School at Statesboro in 1915. During the time he was out of school, and before he entered the University he served in the World War as a machine gun instructor. After returning from the army he was employed as an automobile salesman, at which occupation he proved very successful. At the University he has the distinction of being a Rehabilitation student, although he registered "special" when entering, he soon removed his conditions, and is now a full fledged senior. He has taken an interest in nearly every activity at the University, uniting with the Literary Societies, and playing a "hot" game of politics but never neglecting his studies as evidenced by the fact that he has an average among the five highest in the class. May he spend his life among those who are deserving of such friendship.

name: George Frederick Dugger, LL.B.
motto: "Be honest, do your duty, no matter who it affects, and though the Kings of Corruption unite against you, you will prevail."
description: George is a soldier, a scholar, and a gentleman. His ability as a soldier was exemplified in France where for distinguished valor he rose from a private to a First Lieutenant, received the Croix-de-Guerre, and was cited by general Pershing, Marshall Petain, and General Lejeune. The records of the Law School vouch for his scholarship. His keenness of perception together with his faculty for logical thought, and splendid application to his work, have enabled him to graduate with honors. No one can come in contact with George without realizing that he is a true gentleman. Of course no one can tell what the future will bring forth, but after knowing George intimately for three years—and is there a better test—his classmates are confident of his marked success at the bar.

name: Wallace Houseal Epting, LL.B.
motto: "Regardless of what people think or say, I listen to the dictates of my conscience."
description: "Doc" came to us fresh from the salt zephyrs of "old Chatham," however, it took him only a few short months to lose this freshness under the iron hand of the "old marster." He has proven to be a good student, and what is more to be desired has proven that one may also work hard and still make loyal friends. In college activities, particularly of the political sort, "Doc" has always been active, and few men have been elected to offices of any import without the support of our politician hero. "Doc" is ever a warm friend, a bitter fighter, but when needs be he can bow to defeat with a smile.

name: Robert Spurgeon Florence, LL.B.
motto: "The world in which we live is the world which God chose to create."
description: Realizing the vastness of opportunity facing a college student, yet at the same time keen to futility of a mundane existence, Spurgeon Florence after completing his preparatory training at Richmond Academy, looked forward to the years forming the period of time which would change him into Florence the Man, and resolved that he would become Florence, "A Man of the World." He set about to gather the lore of divergent experiences with which to season the dry intensive knowledge from text books.

name: James Francis Glass, LL.B.
motto: "Do not put a thing off—put it over."
description: The beautious and fair lad from Chatham. James left home. Of course he left it, he couldn't take it with him. On the face of existing conditions, he came to the University of Georgia, so that he might give the young ladies of that institution an appreciation of his facial magnificence, which is distinctive, due to a protruding proboscis, which occurs immediately on the horizon of his physiognomy and contours off into sylph-like curves of angelic abtiquity. Although he is handicapped with these pulchritudinous features, he has proved himself half-way clever in the pursuit of scholastic qualifications.

name: Charles Latimer Gowen, LL.B.
motto: "When you come to the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on."
description: This is Charlie. Charlie is an industrious sort of fellow and quite a politician. However, it is obvious that more honors have been thrust upon him resultant from his industry than from his vote-urging. His forensic ability was proven by the fact that he was chosen as the best orator in the 1925 "Who's Who" at the University. But Charlie is at his best as a manager. He is Business Manager of the book you are now soiling with your fingerprints and had it not been for his activity in that capacity this book would have been delayed much longer. Now he will probably go back to Brunswick and set up a law factory. And he will do well, for after several years under Sylvanus he "has it in him."

name: Rupert Bain Griffith, LL.D.
motto: "Every man gets what he deserves, but only the successful admit it."
description: "When Sylvy's last lecture is over and the diplomas are sealed and signed, we shall rest; and faith we shall need it." Thus spake Griff one morning during the struggle to mount the second hill of Blackstone. Since that time the members of the entire law school have been heartily concurring with him on that point. Aside from the humorous vein that may be hidden in his expression, it does bespeak his true nature, for Griff is an untiring worker. But we don't believe that he will rest when the "Diplomas are sealed and signed," for he has made it a habit, and we'll bet our bottom dollar that Griff will be hard at work again before June is out. Somehow we just don't believe that he would be satisfied if he didn't. But withal he is never too busy, or in such a rush that he doesn't have time for a friendly greeting, a smile, and perhaps a bull session.

name: Albon Lee Hatcher, LL.B.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Law Class
Solicitor General of Jeffersonian Moot Court
description: This beautiful young masculine Cleopatra holds the proud distinction of being one of Georgia's most famous ladies' men, - a heart breaker from his toe nails to his hair tips. He is the type who falls in love with one girl one week and forgets her the next. Algernon will receive his long contemplated degree in law during commencement and according to present plans, he will immediately start himself toward a barrister's career. He will thus wind up a four-year sojourn at the University. The first of these he spent in studying A.B. work, while the latter three were consumed with his efforts to win a legal degree. Blessed with a sparkling and radiating personality, and all kinds of forensic ability Algernon is sure to carve a high niche in Georgia lawyers' hall of fame.

name: Israel Clinton Helmly, Jr., LL.B.
motto: "Nothing is impossible to a willing mind."
description: "Zip" came to us three years ago from "the town of lawyers" and since then has become a well-known and popular figure about the Law School. He has been a steady, consistent and popular student as is testified by his membership in several honorary and social clubs. He has always been ready to lend a helping hand to his many friends or to argue on any question, whether it concerns a sack of salt or the U. S. Constitution. On the athletic field his efficiency and business-like management of teams has shown that here was executive talent of the first calibre. A friend of rare flavor; a worker of ability, persistence and fairness. As "Zip" returns to the State of Chatham -- whether as a lawyer or business man -- his many friends send their best wishes for a future which will live up to the past.

name: Willie D. Holliday, LL.B.
description: From the town of Crawford W. Long and other noted characters comes "Lil' Willie." Oh well, he may not become so famous as his fellow townsmen, in fact we wouldn't dare make such a prediction, for we can never see the other side of the balance which is held unscrupulously in the iron hand of fate. But well may we believe that with such an environment it will be only natural that he should after a while do something that will make us all glad that we knew him, and that we have associated with him here in college. "D" says that women do have their charms. And he firmly believes that the "time he has lost in wooing, in chasing and pursuing the look that lies in women's eyes has been his heart's undoing." In his work here he has done well, and we are waiting to hear of the time when he scores a "knockout."

name: Edith Elizabeth House, LL.B.
clubs: Finance Chairman Y. W. C. A., '22-'23
Undergraduate Representative, Y. W. C. A., '23-'24 and '24-'25
description: Edith is the one exception that proves the rule. She has shown that she is a man among men; that's when we think of her as "Pete," and she has demonstrated that she is a woman among women, that's when we call her "Sister." Her repertoire extends from the role of a young lawyer in clothes of an extremely tailored cut, and tortoise-rimmed spectacles, to the glorified blonde in shimmering evening finery—a toast mistress at a banquet if you please. Her trail of glory extends from Winder High School through the University of Georgia. The itinerary includes many coveted honors; membership in everything, president of nearly everything and delegate to every other thing. A true daughter of the Alma Mater, active and alert.

name: Martin Latimer Johnson, LL.B.
motto: "Our wealth is measured by the amount of good we do in the world."
description: Many of the University's noted sons have hailed from the Classic City, but none deserving more credit than Martin Latimer Johnson. During his high school days which were spent in Athens, he acquired the nickname "Toby" and naturally it has stuck with him. Why he has this title no one seems to know. It may be that he is unlucky, but we who have seen him succeed not only in his pursuit of a fair maiden, and in his scholastic work, but also as a salesman of every make of automobile known to man, all agree that if he is unlucky, more power to him, for he has triumphed over all obstacles. A brighter mind never graced the halls of the Lumpkin Law School. And whether in after years he be known as "Colonel Johnson" or "Salesman Sam" we predict a great success. We are all for you, Martin, old boy.

name: Freeman Napier Jelks, LL.B.
motto: "'Tis better to be a fool with the crowd, than wise by one's self."
enrolltime: came to us from the University of Virginia. After two years of academic work
description: Freeman Napier Jelks, of Hawkinsville, came to us from the University of Virginia. After two years of academic work we find him studying law, and now we see him about to enter the forum. Freeman has excellently and most creditably distinguished himself in his many-sided activities at the University, and all who don't know Freeman know "Tarzan" for he has become immensely popular in University circles.

name: Louis De Blois Milledge, LL.B.
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you; play the piano and you play alone."
description: De Blois decided that he was not temperamentally adapted for the profession of engineering so he left Tech and took up the study of law at Georgia. He has had no cause to regret the change for he has made a good record. The starvation period of a young lawyer holds no great appeal for him so it is likely that posterity will know him as a Morgan rather than as a Blackstone. As Dr. Morris says, "a little knowledge of the law is a mighty handy thing" even for a Captain of Industry. Milledge D's extra-curriculum business activities, which range from matching pennies with Abe Nathan to real estate options, clearly indicate that he has no intention of dying in a poor house. His congenial nature and ready wit have won him many sincere friendships; sincere enough to stand even the test of his musical propensities. The Delt house will not be quite the same next year.

name: Stanley Milledge, LL.B.
description: Behold the countenance of one of Georgia's fairest and most renowned sons. Rich with honors and merits, he leaves behind him convincing evidence, to those who follow, of the rewards of virtue and hard work. No task was too hard for him, for he loved to do things which seemed impossible; none too easy due to thoroughness and attention to details. He comes to us a lawyer and leaves a greater one. A man, who intends to leave his mark on the sands of time; and one who cannot fail to accomplish his task. And, true to traditions, along with mentality and real manhood, has been allotted him, a desire to pacify the romantic propensities of the fairer sex, and which desire has been wholly satisfied. We admit this. He allows it and the ladies acclaim it.

name: Oscar Francis Miller, LL.B.
motto: "Do right, and fear no man; don't write, and fear no woman."
description: Mooney came to us from Milwaukee, the frozen north, after completing his grammar and high school education. He states that the sunny Southland held an irresistible attraction for him. Mooney has won for himself a host of friends during his four year's sojourn in the "classic city". His ready wit and quick sallies are the envy of every bull session; his personality, like a powerful magnet, attracts those who come in contact with him; his friends admit that he is true blue. Mooney has applied himself diligently, both in his books and in the pursuit of worldly knowledge. Though not a confirmed woman hater Mooney does not lavish his affections on the fair sex. It is doubtful whether he will choose law as a profession, but any career he follows, success is bound to be his. Luck to you, my son.

name: Abraham Aaron Nathan, LL.B.
description: Mr. Nathan is rounding out three years as a pursuer of legal knowledge in the Lumpkin Law School. During his career as a law student he has had his ups and downs and pathos and glories just like all students have but we find him still in the ring, his head "bloody but unbowed" and his hopes pointed for the contemplated reception of his LL.B. in Junetime. Abe, as our present hero is known by his many friends and admirers has been one of the best-dressed boys in college, possessing probably the most comprehensive wardrobe of any of his compatriots. He has been in the habit of changing suits three times a day for many years and 'tis said he can swap raiments as quickly as any professional "quick-change artist." Maybe he'll be one after his graduation. Who knows? At any rate the best wishes of his admirers and friends at Georgia will go with him when he steps into the business world.

name: O'Neal, Marvin
description: Marvin during his college career has advanced from the bald-headed stage symbolic of ignorance to the position of trust and confidence symbolized by the traditional cane and derby. He is well versed in the principles of law having submerged victorious and battle scarred as a result of frequent encounters with "Battling Sylvia". Aside from the honors listed above, he possesses the priceless ingredients, honor and integrity, plus the ability to make and keep friends. With all his even disposition and genial nature, he is fearless and aggressive, having won from these qualities the names of statesman, four-forty, and champion shadow-boxer. His friends and well wishers at the University are limited only because the small enrollment therein; in short he is a man among men, a lion among the ladies. The world is your oyster Marvin. Go open it.

name: Perry, Jr., Terrel Rainey
motto: "Always be true to the best that is in you."
description: Six years have materially changed the boy who came up to us from South Georgia. In this time by hard work and consistent industry, "Commodore" has obtained two degrees and many honors. In all of his activities he has shown a conscientiousness and ability that promises well for his future success. His friends have found that he is willing and eager to lend a helping hand at any time. Although much criticism has come his way, a fair-minded person must admit that he has extended all his energy in trying to make the University bigger and better socially. He has sterling qualities which any observer may easily find, his only weakness being the girls. These the average student must have. We will all miss him very much, and earnestly wish him great success.

name: SAFFOLD, ROBERT EARLE
motto: "I hope that Hell is as pleasant as the road that leads to it."
description: THERE are a lot of lawyers WHO are literally soothsayers, BECAUSE they know that the TINKLING of the cymbal is PLEASING to the ear. THEIR system is BASED on the principle of Barnum AND after all their customers COME only once. AND these clients will beat a PATH to Bob's office for THEIR next advice. WHICH reminds us that a BURNT child is afraid of fire.

name: SMITH, JUDSON BUTTS
description: Success has crowned Jud's endeavor. Three years have made him an acknowledged leader on the campus and off. The incarnation of modesty, he has sought few college honors, yet some of those really worth while have been thrust upon him. Many attain success, Jud has done that and something which few do. He has deserved it. With Jud there is no compromise with right. This has beset his path with some unpleasant difficulties from which he has always emerged with the respect of his antagonist if not with his well wishes. When one enters the great school of life it is usual to wish him luck. With Jud we shall not do this. He needs no luck. But, we shall be happy when, as he certainly shall, he attains all the success that's safe to man. That is also real friendship and we shall end our say with these words: "A truer friend never lived to bless the lives of men." If you are right with yourself you are right with the world, and the world will eventually judge you so.

name: Aaron Joselove
motto: "The respect you have for yourself will determine the respect others have for you."
description: Aaron came to us from the land of eternal youth, where it's always summer and during his stay has shown us some hot capers on the grid and featured youth with his ability. Equipped with a physique that would have been a source of envy even to the ancient Grecian gods he has established himself as the athlete you read about in books as well as his actions on fields of encounter have given him the qualifications you must have read about in the newspapers. But Aaron is not only an athlete; he is also a scholar and a man of considerable social prestige. And with him on the ball room floor it is pretty much the same story as on the gridiron. He has not sought honors; thus they have been thrust upon him.
